You will have frequent internal contacts with Nordic Sales and Marketing, as well as our headquarters in South Korea and external partners, making you an important part of Samsung’s continuing growth in innovative display solutions. You will be working in our Office in Kista; the role includes travel in the Nordic and Europe. You will report to Head of Product Management - Peter Eklund.
   

  

**Role And Responsibilities**
 You will be working with:
   

  

* Full responsibility managing one of our display product areas on the Nordic market.
* Overseeing the complete product lifecycle: from portfolio selection and pricing to go-to-market strategy, promotional rollout, and performance tracking.
* Responsibility for securing target KPI’s such as net sales, profit, market shares, sell-in/sell out forecasting
* Collaborating daily with Nordic Sales and Marketing, HQ in South Korea, and external partners.
* Supporting the sales organization by applying your analytical skills and service-oriented mindset.
* Handling administrative tasks and ensuring detail-oriented execution in a fast-paced environment.
* Taking own initiatives to adapt to changes and drive growth in an organization with a high tempo.


**Skills And Qualifications**
 In the role, it is important that you have/are:
   

  

* A recent Degree or related program in Business, Marketing, or Engineering.
* 0-2 years’ experience in a related role.
* Great Excel & PowerPoint skills.
* Strong analytical and administrative skills, with a detail-oriented and flexible mindset.
* A service-oriented team player with excellent social skills, thriving in a dynamic sales organization.
* Self-driven with an appreciation for a high tempo, continuous change, and taking own initiatives.
* Fluency in English and Swedish.


 Samsung is a dynamic company in a fast-moving industry, and there are many opportunities to grow and develop! To enjoy working with us you must be a person who appreciates tempo, change, and taking own initiatives.
   

  

 Selection and interview process is ongoing; please apply as soon as possible but no later than 2026-05-30. We look forward to getting your application!
   

  

**#** 
**LifeAtSamsung**
**Samsung Fast Facts**
**#DoWhatYouCant**
**All information shared during the recruitment process will be handled with strict confidentiality, any trade secrets or proprietary details will be protected in accordance with applicable laws and company policies.**
* Please visit Samsung membership to see Privacy Policy, which defaults according to your location, at: https://account.samsung.com/membership/policy/privacy. You can change Country/Language at the bottom of the page. If you are European Economic Resident, please click here: https://europe-samsung.com/ghrp/PrivacyNoticeforEU.html

title: Lead Solution Architect
QA Test Engineer — Machine Control Division
description:
**Do you want to be part of a global team digitizing the heavy construction industry and contributing to a more sustainable world?** 




 At Leica Geosystems Technology, we are looking for a QA Test Engineer for our Machine Control Division. We are seeking a highly professional Test Engineer with solid experience in a testing role. The ideal candidate will have expertise in manual testing across multiple levels, with a strong focus on improving testing processes and delivering high-quality software.
 



  





**About the role** 




 You will be part of the On-Machine Software department, which designs and develops machine control software for heavy construction machines, with a primary focus on Excavators. Your work will span from embedded systems to our Leica MC1 machine control application — the operator-facing platform that guides and automates machines directly from the cabin. All our software runs on specialized hardware mounted on real machines.
 



 Your day-to-day will be varied — from in-office feature testing and regression testing on our local T-Site, to getting out in the field and testing software on real excavators under real working conditions. You will also be involved in requirements and design, shaping features before they're built.
 



 You will be directly embedded in a software Scrum team as the team's dedicated test engineer, working alongside Software Engineers, Product Specialists, and UI/UX specialists. The software team is based in Stockholm, while you will be working from our office in Odense, Denmark. This means you will have a high degree of autonomy in your day-to-day work, while being well-supported by experienced QA colleagues on-site in Odense and staying closely connected with your Scrum team through daily collaboration.
 



  





**Key responsibilities** 



* **Quality from the Start:** 
 Participate in feature planning, requirements refinement, and design discussions — helping the team define clear acceptance criteria and test scenarios before a single line of code is written.
* **Sprint Planning & Estimation:** 
 Help the Scrum team break down and estimate work with a quality perspective, ensuring testability and risk are considered early.
* **Software & System Testing:** 
 Plan, execute, and lead both functional and non-functional testing, including regression and performance testing — in-office as well as on construction machinery at test sites.
* **Documentation:** 
 Create and maintain test plans, test cases, and detailed test documentation that is clear and concise.
* **Process Improvement:** 
 Contribute to the continuous improvement of QA processes, methodologies, and test strategies across both SAFe and Scrum teams.



  





**Your profile** 




 You have a relevant tertiary education — a bachelor's degree or equivalent in engineering, surveying, computer science, or a related technical field — or equivalent hands-on commercial experience. What matters most is that you are curious, hands-on, and genuinely interested in construction machinery and the technology behind it. We're looking for someone who is self-driven and collaborative — eager to take ownership of their work while being a great teammate.
 







**We expect you to have:** 



* Solid experience in a testing or quality assurance role
* Experience working in agile teams with iterative development and regular delivery cycles
* Experience in creating and maintaining test documentation (test plans, test cases, reports)
* Strong problem-solving skills, interpersonal skills, and attention to detail
* Excellent communication skills, both verbal and written, with the ability to collaborate across departments and with stakeholders at various levels
* Comfortable working independently and taking ownership of your testing responsibilities, while proactively seeking collaboration with both your remote Scrum team and local QA colleagues



**Desired skills:** 



* ISTQB or other relevant certifications
* Exposure to JIRA or similar project tracking tools
* Experience in machine control software or the construction industry; ability to comfortably operate construction machines is a big plus
* Experience working in Linux environments
* Field test experience, particularly setting up field test environments
* Awareness of test automation concepts and tools — while our current focus is on manual testing, we have ambitions to introduce automated testing in the coming years, and an interest in helping shape that journey is welcome



  





**Language:** 
 Fluent English, both written and verbal, is a prerequisite. Additional languages are an advantage.
 



  





**Travel:** 
 Occasional travel may be required, for example to visit test sites or collaborate with the Stockholm team.

title: Data Analyst
description:
Mentimeter is an engagement tool with a clear goal in mind. To turn presentations into conversations. Through real-time interactivity and clear visualizations, we get people to participate, engage and become more productive. Transforming all those passive meetings, airless classrooms and drawn out trainings into valuable and memorable moments.
   

  

 We truly believe that you achieve the best results by doing things together. And that successful leaders need to adopt a curious and collaborative mindset in order to get there. So with you at Mentimeter, you’ll be a big part of the ambition to help over 1 billion people listen, learn and work better together.
   

  

 Are you passionate about leveraging data to drive business impact and take analytics to the next level? Join Mentimeter as our
 **Data Analyst** 
 , where you will set new standards for our analytics craft within the Data & Analytics (DNA) team and play a pivotal role in advancing our analytics capabilities to drive impactful, data-informed strategies for our SaaS-based business. Data is not only central, but crucial, to how Mentimeter will reach success.
   

  

 At Mentimeter, the Data & Analytics team is on a mission to catalyse our company strategy and drive innovation, growth and impact throughout our company and user journey by using data. We work at the intersection of analytics, data science, and business to deliver actionable insights and solutions that enable better decisions across departments, including Product, Marketing, Sales, and Revenue Operations. Your contributions will directly influence key business outcomes such as customer retention, engagement, and expansion.
   

  

 In this role, you’ll collaborate with and be supported by cross-functional teams of data scientists, analytics engineers, user researchers, product managers and revenue leaders who are passionate about Mentimeter’s mission and using data to achieve it.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
* Partner with senior leaders and stakeholders to define key questions, success metrics, and analytical approaches that enable strategic decisions.
* Deliver high-impact analyses and decision support that connect insights across our functions and company.
* Be a key part of our Analytics team to align priorities and ensure solutions are relevant and impactful.
* Collaborate closely with Analytics Engineering and other close teams to enable the business to proactively act on data and create business impact.
* Elevate the analytics craft by setting new standards, implementing innovative approaches and best practices.
* Act as a trusted thought partner and mentor to analysts and stakeholders alike, raising analytical maturity across the organization.
* Translate complex business questions and analytical findings into compelling narratives and visualizations that resonate with diverse audiences.


**We believe you have**
* A minimum of 5 years of relevant work experience.
* Exceptional communication skills, with the ability to convey complex concepts through clear narratives and visualizations.
* Strong business acumen and experience applying analytics within a domain such as product, sales or marketing.
* Proven expertise in analytics and data exploration, with proficiency in tools such as SQL and BI/visualization platforms.
* A builder mindset and interest to create analysis and systems that scale, leveraging automation, generative AI, and data to drive Mentimeter’s growth and efficiency.
* Comfort working with analytics engineering practices and collaborating with data engineers on data models and pipelines.
* A track record of delivering high-quality, actionable insights aligned with business objectives.
* Experience partnering closely with senior stakeholders and leadership teams.


**Nice to have**
* Familiarity with modern data tools (e.g. Looker, Hex, Snowflake).
* Experience applying AI in analytical workflows, such as setting up AI agents or automation to accelerate analysis, insight generation, and decision support.
* Experience working within a SaaS environment, with a strong understanding of the unique business challenges and opportunities it presents.


**Not Required**
* Swedish is not required (as daily work is carried out in English and the Mentimeter team currently boasts 50+ different nationalities)


**What Mentimeter Can Offer**
 At Mentimeter we can offer a diverse and inclusive work environment supported by smart and driven colleagues. We believe in continuous professional development for all of our colleagues and therefore offer access to a leadership program (including external personal coach) and relevant education to ensure that we continue to be state-of-the-art when it comes to innovating and building Mentimeter. Your place will be in a growing company with lots of career opportunities, working on a beloved product used by more than 300 million people. It’s not all about work though, we also offer a very healthy view on work-life balance.
   

  

 All of this comes attached with a competitive compensation and benefits package, including pension contributions. Learn more about our benefits by visiting our Benefits & Perks page
   

  

**AI and Hiring at Mentimeter**
 At Mentimeter, we believe AI helps us work smarter - but it never replaces the human assessment, curiosity, and personal connection that define our culture and our hiring. We use AI as a sparring partner: to bounce ideas, bring new perspectives, support structure, and make our work more efficient. But the meaning, decisions, and interactions always come from people.
   

  

* AI does not screen or decide on candidates.
* There is no automated filtering, ranking, or decision-making in our recruitment process. Every application is reviewed by a person.
* Hiring teams may use AI to support their work - for example, to structure notes, prepare interview questions, or organize their thinking.


 AI strengthens our work, but it does not define it. At Mentimeter, we’re not building an AI-driven hiring process - we’re building a people-first culture, where technology helps us listen, learn, and grow together.
   

  

**Culture at Mentimeter**
 At Mentimeter we believe in giving everyone a voice - regardless of who you are. So we build a platform that does just that. Our platform is not only our product but also our organization. A platform where people feel safe, where differences are embraced, a place where you can have fun. We strongly encourage applicants who are people of color, LGBTQ+, women, people with disabilities, and/or formerly incarcerated people, and a college degree is not strictly required. In order to give everyone a voice, we need to be as diverse as our users.
   

  

 Learn more about our culture by visiting our Culture page.
   

  

 Review our Privacy Policy for more information.

title: Senior Network Security Engineer
description:
**About Justera Group**
 Justera Group AB is a leading Swedish IT consulting firm with great experience specializing in building high-performing technology teams and delivering end-to-end IT solutions. Led by dynamic female executives, Justera Group focuses on helping businesses harness the power of digital technologies to continuously evolve in today’s rapidly changing economy. The company has deep expertise in IT consultancy, software development, and recruitment services, providing clients with purpose-driven, well-trained consultants who consistently deliver efficient and effective results. Justera Group manages the entire talent lifecycle, from hiring to ongoing management, ensuring the right fit for each project on time and within budget. With their strong commitment to quality, collaboration, and client success, Justera Group is a trusted partner for technology innovation across Sweden.
   

  

**About the Role:**
 Justera is currently recruiting a Senior Network Security Engineer (Level 3) on behalf of one of our clients, a global leader in industrial innovation. This is a critical, high-impact position based at the client’s headquarters in Södertälje.
   

  

 As an on-site L3 expert, you will be the senior technical point of contact for enterprise-grade Fortinet firewall operations. You will sit at the heart of the network security team, bridging the gap between operational support and high-level architecture. This role is designed for a "hands-on" expert who thrives on resolving complex, cross-domain incidents and ensuring the absolute stability of a mission-critical security environment.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities:**
* Advanced Operational Support: Resolve complex security incidents involving Fortinet infrastructure, network overlays/underlays, and BGP/OSPF routing connectivity.
* Deep Diagnostics: Perform expert-level packet inspection, session-level troubleshooting, and system diagnostics for HA (High Availability) clusters.
* Change Management: Design and execute non-standard, high-risk changes aligned with HLD/LLD templates and ITIL frameworks.
* Service Optimization: Lead Root Cause Analysis (RCA) for recurring issues and proactively identify architectural improvements to enhance resilience and security posture.
* Vendor & Architecture Liaison: Act as the primary escalation point for Fortinet TAC and work closely with internal L4 Architects on project onboarding and design reviews.
* Mentorship: Provide technical guidance and best practices to L1 and L2 support teams.


**Mandatory Technical Qualifications:**
* Experience: Minimum 5+ years of experience operating enterprise-grade security environments (Data Center focus).
* Certification: Must hold a valid Fortinet Certified Professional (FCP) or NSE 4-6 equivalent (Credly badge or PDF proof required).
* Fortinet Stack: Proven hands-on mastery of FortiGate, FortiManager, FortiAnalyzer, and SD-WAN.
* Network Fundamentals: Strong command of Layer 2–7 packet analysis, vulnerability remediation, and multi-domain traffic flows.


**Preferred (Nice-to-Have):**
* Fortinet Certified Solution Specialist (FCSS/NSE 7).
* Experience with Check Point firewalls (CCSA or CCSE certifications).
* Familiarity with SIEM and NMS monitoring platforms.


**Logistics:**
* Location: 100% Onsite at Södertälje Headquarters.
* Type: Full-time, Permanent.
* Schedule: Local operational hours, with availability for extended support during major incidents or planned maintenance windows.
* Reporting: Reports to the Network Security Operations Manager with a dotted line to the L4 Architecture team.


**What We Offer:**
* A dynamic and innovative workplace driving advancements in cutting-edge robotic technologies
* The opportunity to collaborate with talented cross-functional teams on meaningful and impactful projects
* Competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits package
* A supportive culture that fosters continuous learning, growth, and professional development
* Flexible hybrid working model for an optimal work-life balance
* 25 days of annual leave to recharge and relax
* An annual wellness allowance of 3,500 SEK to support your health and wellbeing

title: IT Project Manager
description:
We are looking for an IT Project Manager to join us at Telia Cygate.
   

  

 My name is Jelena Lazarevic, and I am responsible for the Project Management Team at Telia Cygate Region East. I’m now looking for someone who wants to grow together with us. We are a supportive and forward‑looking team with a strong focus on delivering customer IT infrastructure projects with high quality, clear communication, and a strong sense of ownership.
   

  

 In this role, you will work in complex customer environments where structure, transparency, and trust are key. You are someone who takes initiative, enjoys responsibility, and feels comfortable driving projects forward independently. You are confident in coordinating stakeholders, setting direction in your projects, and ensuring that progress keeps moving in the right direction.
   

  

**What you’ll do:**
* Plan, drive, and manage customer projects from start to finish, ensuring delivery according to scope, timeline, and budget.
* Take ownership of your projects and proactively follow up on risks, dependencies, and changes.
* Ensure high quality, transparency, and customer satisfaction throughout the project lifecycle
* Act as the main point of contact for customers and coordinate closely with sales and the delivery organization.
* Facilitate clear communication between technical teams and non‑technical stakeholders.
* Contribute to continuous improvement in how we work with project execution and collaboration.


**What will help you thrive in this role:**
* Proven experience in Project Management, preferably in IT, infrastructure, or technology‑driven environments.
* A proactive and independent way of working - you take responsibility and move things forward.
* Strong communication skills and the ability to adapt your message to different stakeholders and target groups.
* A structured and solution‑oriented mindset, with the ability to handle complexity and change.
* A basic understanding of IT solutions, systems, and infrastructure.
* Fluent communication skills in Swedish and English.
* Experience working as a consultant is an advantage.
* Project management certifications are an advantage.


**Why Telia Cygate?** 
  

  

 At Telia Cygate, we’re not only experts in networks, security, and various types of cloud solutions – we’re also a team that believes work should be fun! As part of Telia Company, we have the strength to create real impact on a large scale, contribute to societal value, and help our customers navigate the world of digitalization. And we always do it with warmth, care, and a smile. Here, it’s our ideas and diverse perspectives that make us strong, and together we ensure that the world of technology becomes a place where everyone feels good – and where you belong.
   

  

 Your development matters to us. We encourage continuous learning and make sure that you and your interests are in focus. We know that people are the heart of our company, which is why we value joy, creativity, and different perspectives in everything we do.
   

  

 Welcome to grow together with us – where we make technology human and create real impact!
   

  

**Telia Cygate is proud to be certificated as Great Place to Work!** 
  

  

 With us, people are at the center – here you get the chance to grow, be challenged, and thrive together with dedicated colleagues. We are building a culture where security, togetherness, and opportunities go hand in hand. Do you want to be part of the journey? Welcome to a workplace that truly lives up to its name.
   

  

**What we offer** 
  

  

 When you join us, you'll receive a comprehensive benefits package tailored to your local market, opportunities for remote work, and more. You'll also have the chance to earn a bonus based on your performance and Telia's results.
   

  

**Interested? Apply!**
 We look forward to receiving your application by
 **2026-05-24.** 
 Please note that we might close the position earlier if we find the right candidate, so don’t wait too long to apply!
   

  

 Location:
 **Solna**
 If you’re selected for the next stage, you’ll be contacted by our
 **Talent Acquisition Partner.**
 Background and reference checks will be part of the process.
   

  

 Want to know more about this role? Please reach out to Jelena Lazarevic at jelena.lazarevic@cygate.se.
   

  

 We kindly ask recruitment agencies and external vendors not to contact us regarding this position.
   

  

**Equal opportunities**
 At Telia, we recognize talent by professional skills and growth potential. We champion diversity, ensuring every individual’s needs are met, regardless of disability, gender, age, nationality, sexual orientation, or social status. Together, we’re dedicated to fostering an inclusive work environment where your uniqueness is our strength.
   

  

**Welcome to Telia Cygate – Home of Experts**

title: Head of Applications
description:
**Sista ansökningsdatum:** 
 17 maj, 2026
   

  

*Tyréns Group is a multidisciplinary consultancy targeting market leadership and delivery of sustainable solutions. We are specialists in designing cities, the built environment and infrastructure solutions.*
 Tyréns Group are strengthening Group IT across Sweden, the UK and Central Europe into one organisation serving the whole business. We are now looking for a Head of Applications to lead and develop this area at Group level.
   

  

 This is an opportunity to shape an important area within Tyréns Group at a time when direction, structure and leadership matter. You will join a knowledge-driven organisation with experienced colleagues, a collaborative culture and a long-term perspective.
   

  

**About The Role**
 This is a senior leadership role within Group IT. You will be part of the Group IT Leadership Team and have overall responsibility for the Group's application landscape, including strategy, governance, lifecycle management, application delivery, software asset management and vendor management.
   

  

 We are looking for a leader who can combine strategic direction with operational grip and work closely with business leaders, enterprise architecture, cybersecurity, procurement and other parts of Group IT to ensure that the application landscape is secure, well-governed, cost-effective and aligned with business needs.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Strategy and governance: Define and drive a Group-wide approach to application strategy, lifecycle management and governance, with appropriate alignment to enterprise architecture, cybersecurity and data governance.
* Leadership: Lead and develop the teams working with application strategy, application delivery, software asset management and vendor management, and create clear priorities, roles and ways of working.
* Application lifecycle landscape: Build a more coherent, well-governed and cost-effective application lifecycle landscape, balancing Group-wide standards with the needs of the business.
* Delivery and operations: Oversee licensing, renewals, compliance, optimisation, packaging, testing, deployment, and ongoing performance of the application landscape.
* Harmonisation: Continue the harmonisation of processes, tools and governance across Sweden, the UK and Central Europe, in close dialogue with regional stakeholders and business leaders.
* Stakeholder management: Work closely with business leaders, procurement, architecture, cybersecurity and other parts of Group IT to prioritise needs and ensure applications support value creation in the business.
* Leadership team contribution: Contribute actively as a member of the Group IT Leadership Team and help shape the wider direction of Group IT.


**We offer**
 As an employee at Tyréns, we offer you, among other things:
   

  

* Great colleagues and many specialists across our different areas of expertise
* A values-driven culture where Connect, Create and Challenge are our guiding principles
* A stimulating work environment in our offices, with the opportunity to work from home for part of your working time
* Opportunities to work on innovation projects
* Professional development tailored to you
* Social activities, both during and outside regular working hours
* Additional paid days off beyond your annual leave—for example, we are always off with pay between Christmas and New Year
* A wide range of employee benefits through our benefits portal
* Collective agreement with Innovationsföretagen (Almega)


 Click
 *here* 
 to read more about us, the types of projects we work on, the development opportunities available, our innovation initiatives, our blogs, and to listen to our employees’ podcasts.
   

  

**Your background**
* Experience: Senior experience from leading an applications function, application management team or a closely related IT service area.
* Domain expertise: Proven experience of software asset management, licensing, vendor relationships and application delivery.
* Change and alignment: Experience of harmonising processes, tools or governance across organisational boundaries and, ideally, across countries or business units.
* Business understanding: Experience from engineering, technical consulting or another project-based knowledge business is an advantage.
* Language: Comfortable operating in English in an international setting. Knowledge of Swedish is a requirement.


**Personal qualities**
 We are looking for a mature and steady leader who is comfortable operating in a senior role. You combine strategic thinking with operational grip, and you are able to create clarity in a complex environment.
   

  

* Judgement and leadership: You are comfortable making decisions, taking ownership and handling difficult questions when needed. You can challenge constructively, escalate when necessary and keep the long-term interests of the business in mind.
* Structure and pragmatism: You bring order without creating unnecessary bureaucracy, and you know how to turn broad intent into workable governance and clear delivery.
* Technical credibility: You are able to discuss applications, architecture, lifecycle issues and delivery models with specialists and senior stakeholders alike. You do not need to be the deepest expert in the room, but you do need the credibility to lead the area with confidence.
* Collaboration: You build trust, listen well and make good use of the expertise around you. You are comfortable working across organisational boundaries, cultures and time zones.
* Inclusive leadership: You create an environment where people can contribute, develop and be heard, and you value different perspectives and ways of working.


**Apply**
 You are welcome to submit your application. A cover letter is not required.
   

  

 We recruit on an ongoing basis, so please submit your application today, as the position may be filled before the application deadline.
   

  

 In accordance with GDPR, we do not accept applications by email. We only process applications submitted via the application link.
   

  

**Contact**
 If you would like to know more about the role or have any questions, you are welcome to contact:
   

  

 Olof Dedering
   

  

 CIO
   

  

 Mail: olof.dedering@tyrens.se
   

  

 Phone: +46761900984
   

  

 Tyréns is one of Sweden’s leading community development consultancies. Together with our customers and partners, we create sustainable solutions in the fields of urban development and infrastructure. Tyréns Group has 3 000 employees with operations in Sweden, United Kingdom, Lithuania, Estonia, Poland, Bulgaria and Canada. Tyréns is owned by a private foundation and this makes it possible to commit ourselves to research and innovation within the urban and rural development sector. We are driven by a thirst for knowledge and work closely with universities and other research institutes.

title: 1st Line Support
location: Upplands Väsby och Sollentuna, Stockholm County, Sweden
description:
Nu söker vi engagerade kundservicemedarbetare på heltid med erfarenhet av service och ett intresse för teknik. Som kundservicemedarbetare blir du en nyckelperson i att hantera och utveckla kundrelationer samt säkerställa högkvalitativ service varje dag. Dessutom får du möjligheten att ha en betydande inverkan på företagets framtida utveckling.
   

  

 Kontoret är beläget i Rotebro, intill pendeltågsstationen. Här kommer du arbeta på plats tillsammans med 80 kollegor, där cirka 25 av dem tillhör kundserviceteamet. Miljön på Loomis-Pay präglas av en social och välkomnande atmosfär med fokus på samarbete och trivsel. Förutom ett välutrustat gym erbjuds olika gemensamma aktiviteter som bidrar till en stark teamkänsla.
   

  

**Hur du kommer påverka**
 Som Kundservicemedarbetare Kommer Du Att Vara En Del Av Ett Dynamiskt Team Med Ansvar För Att Hantera Ärenden Från Företagskunder. I Din Roll Kommer Du
   

  

* Besvara kundfrågor via telefon och mejl om betalningar, avtal, beställningar, fakturor, felanmälningar samt pris- och transportfrågor.
* Hantera och administrera ärenden i Salesforce.
* Samarbeta med andra avdelningar för att hantera kundärenden.
* Representera Loomis som kundens första kontaktpunkt och säkerställa en positiv kundupplevelse.
* Ge våra kunder teknisk support med företagets produkter och tjänster.


**Vem är du?**
 Vi Söker Dig Som Trivs i En Roll Med Högt Tempo Och Har En Hög Kommunikativ Förmåga Med En Tydlig Serviceanda. Hos Oss Värdesätter Vi Särskilt Dig Som Kan Ta Egna Initiativ Och Ta Dig An Problem På Ett Kreativt Angreppssätt. Vi Ser Även Gärna Att Du Har
   

  

* Tidigare erfarenhet inom kundservice eller liknande roll, gärna i en tekniskt orienterad miljö.
* Stark system- och datorvana; erfarenhet av att arbeta med office-paketet, särskilt Excel.
* Erfarenhet av Salesforce, IFS eller liknande system är meriterande.
* En fullständig gymnasieexamen.
* Flytande kunskaper i svenska och engelska, både i tal och skrift.
* Ett stort intresse för teknik och förmågan att förklara tekniska lösningar på ett pedagogiskt sätt.
* Meriterande om du har tidigare erfarenhet av att ha arbetat med betallösningar, POS och/ eller teknisk support.


**Om oss**
 Loomis—Pay är ett startup och en del av Loomis Group, med målet att erbjuda en all-in-one-betalningslösning för butiker, kaféer och restauranger över hela världen. Sedan lanseringen i oktober 2020 har vi lanserat i tre länder och byggt upp ett team på över 100 medarbetare som arbetar hårt för att göra det till en global framgång. Ta chansen att ansluta dig till en snabbt växande organisation med stark entreprenörsanda!
   

  

**Bli en del av Loomis Pay**
 Är du rätt person för jobbet? Ansök redan idag! Vi granskar ansökningar löpande och sista ansökningsdagen är 2026-05-31. Observera att ansökningar som mottas via e-post eller direktmeddelande inte kommer att accepteras.
   

  

*Vi kommer att genomföra en bakgrundskontroll på den slutgiltiga kandidaten.*

title: License Owner, Stockholm
description:
**Passionate about football (soccer) AND about business? Stranger Soccer is expanding to key cities around the world. We are looking for a License Owner to open and operate the Stranger Soccer business in Stockholm.**
**This is not your traditional management job - it is truly built for someone that wants to own and operate their own business** 
 (that happens to make the world's greatest sport accessible to everyone). This role is ideally suited for an adventurous and football-passionate entrepreneur.
   

  

**Sounds Like You?**
 As a next step, please visit www.strangersoccer.com and particularly the section Bring Stranger Soccer to your City to learn more about this dynamic opportunity.
 **If it appears to be a fit, and you wish to learn more, please fill out the form there to officially express your interest.** 
 Be sure to download and explore the Stranger Soccer mobile app as well.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Bring the Stranger Soccer brand and business to life for the assigned city
* Recruit, train, and lead a small central team (2-3 persons) as well as a few freelance Official Hosts (game hosts) to kick off the operation
* Identify and secure prime slots at football venues to run Stranger Soccer games
* Engage in creative sales and marketing digital and hands-onto ensure that customers are introduced to the platform as a convenient and preferred way to play
* Oversee a schedule of games, ensuring customers are delighted with each game they play, and keep coming back for more
* Take advantage of the strong backing and direction of Singapore headquarters, where an operations and technology team is ready to support you


**You should have**
* A passion for football, and a strong connection to your local football scene
* A business background, ideally in management and customer service
* An entrepreneurial background or spirit
* A commitment to owning, driving, and scaling the Stranger Soccer brand in the assigned market
* A hunger to succeed: financial compensation for this role will be proportionate to hard work, business acumen, and effectiveness


**About Stranger Soccer**
 With 3.5 billion football enthusiasts in over 200 countries, football is the most popular sport in the world. But why don't more people play it? Stranger Soccer (www.strangersoccer.com) makes playing football as easy as going for a jog or to the gym. Browse dozens of games on its acclaimed Mobile App. Book a slot. Turn up and play.
   

  

 Born in Singapore, Stranger Soccer is now open in 10+ cities around the world, with over 100,000 games run. We continue to grow and are accepting applications from license partners who want to bring Stranger Soccer to their city. Whether you call it Fussball, Fútbol, Calcio, or anything else, the world now awaits.
   

  

**Our Mission**
 To make playing football as easy as going for a jog.
   

  

**Our Vision**
 A world in which playing football is as popular and widespread as watching football.
   

  

**Got what it takes?**
**We look forward to meeting you.**

title: Electrical Design Engineer
description:
Cleeven is an Engineering, R&D, and IT consulting group supporting clients in their
 **R&D efforts and operational excellence** 
 . We specialize in innovation and advanced engineering across multiple sectors, including
 **energy, aerospace & defense, transportation, pharmaceuticals & chemicals, and IT & telecoms** 
 .
 



  





 We are currently looking for
 **Electrical Design Engineers** 
 to join our team. In this role, you will be responsible for the
 **design, development, and integration of electrical systems** 
 , ensuring compliance with industry standards and project requirements. You will work closely with multidisciplinary teams to develop high-quality electrical schematics and system layouts for a variety of applications.
 



  





**Your Responsibilities:** 



* **Design and develop electrical systems.**
* Create and modify
 **electrical schematics, wiring diagrams, and system layouts** 
 using industry-standard tools.
* Conduct
 **feasibility studies and technical assessments** 
 to optimize system performance.
* Ensure proper
 **cable routing, circuit protection, and electrical safety compliance** 
 .
* Perform
 **testing, troubleshooting, and validation** 
 of electrical systems.
* Maintain technical documentation and ensure adherence to relevant standards and regulations.



  





**Qualifications:** 



* **Degree in Electrical Engineering** 
 or a related field.
* Experience in
 **electrical system design, schematics development, and wiring layouts** 
 .
* Proficiency with
 **CAD tools** 
 such as
 **EPLAN, COMOS, ElproCAD, AutoCAD, Comos, E3, Engineering base.**
* Knowledge of
 **low and medium-voltage systems, control panels, and distribution networks** 
 .
* Strong analytical skills and attention to detail.
* Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment and manage multiple projects.
* Experience in
 **testing and verification** 
 of electrical systems is a plus.



  





 We are currently looking to fill
 **two positions** 
 : one for a
 **Senior Electrical Design Engineer** 
 and one for a
 **Junior Electrical Design Engineer** 
 .

title: Head of Industrialization and Manufacturing Systems
description:
**Head of Industrialization & Manufacturing Systems** 

  
 




**Location: Nyköping / Stockholm** 

  
 




**Reporting to: Head of Production** 

  
 




**Management team: TBD** 

  
 





  
 




**Role Purpose** 

  
 




**Own and build X Shore’s industrial foundation, ensuring products can be manufactured in a scalable, cost-efficient, and repeatable way.** 

  
 





  
 




**Core Mission** 

  
 




**Build a production system X Shore can trust and scale.** 

  
 





  
 




**Key Responsibilities** 

  
 




**Design and implement scalable production workflows and factory setup** 

  
 




**Standardize work instructions and establish structured production flow** 

  
 




**Identify and remove bottlenecks across the production system** 

  
 




**Implement and own the digital manufacturing backbone (MES)** 

  
 




**Align PLM, ERP, and MES into one coherent system** 

  
 




**Enable real-time production tracking and full traceability per boat** 

  
 




**Establish and own BOM governance, version control, and cost transparency** 

  
 




**Support and drive cost reduction initiatives across the platform** 

  
 




**Define and enforce industrial readiness criteria before production release** 

  
 




**Ensure complete BOM, supplier readiness, and production documentation** 

  
 




**Implement clear release gates between R&D and production** 

  
 




**Structure and own the R&D → production handover process** 

  
 




**Ensure manufacturability is addressed early in development** 

  
 




**Reduce rework through strong integration between engineering and production** 

  
 




**Create feedback loops from production back into R&D** 

  
 





  
 




**Non-Negotiables** 

  
 




**Strong experience building or scaling production systems** 

  
 




**Deep understanding of BOM, manufacturing processes, and cost structures** 

  
 




**Experience with MES, ERP, and PLM systems** 

  
 




**Structured and disciplined execution mindset** 

  
 




**Ability to challenge engineering on manufacturability and cost** 

  
 




**Hands-on approach with focus on practical implementation** 

  
 




**Strong ownership and ability to drive cross-functional alignment** 

  
 





  
 




**What Good Looks Like** 

  
 




**Production is structured, predictable, and repeatable** 

  
 




**Clear BOM ownership and cost transparency across all products** 

  
 




**Minimal rework between R&D and production** 

  
 




**Full visibility of production status and bottlenecks** 

  
 




**Digital systems (MES/ERP/PLM) are aligned and actively used** 

  
 




**Products are released only when fully production-ready** 

  
 




**Strong alignment between R&D, purchasing, and production** 

  
 




**What Bad Looks Like** 

  
 




**Unstructured or inconsistent production processes** 

  
 




**Unclear or changing BOMs** 

  
 




**Frequent rework and late-stage engineering changes** 

  
 




**Lack of visibility into production status** 

  
 




**Disconnected systems (PLM, ERP, MES not aligned)** 

  
 




**Production starts without full readiness** 

  
 




**Ongoing friction between R&D and production** 

  
 





  
 




**Why This Role Matters** 

  
 




**This role is critical to ensuring that X Shore can move from product development to scalable production without cost overruns, delays, or quality issues.** 

  
 




**The right person builds the system that enables the company to produce reliably, at cost, and at scale.** 

  
 





  
 




**Key Interfaces** 

  
 




**Head of R&D – product design, system architecture, and readiness** 

  
 




**System Lead – system requirements and integration impact** 

  
 




**Production / Site Manager – execution, flow, and bottlenecks** 

  
 




**Strategic Purchaser – supplier readiness, cost, and material flow** 

  
 




**Finance – cost structure, working capital, and investment alignment** 

  
 





  
 




**Key Principles** 

  
 




**Build for scale from day one** 

  
 




**No production without full readiness** 

  
 




**Cost is part of the design** 

  
 




**Keep it simple and structured** 

  
 




**Solve root causes, not symptoms**

title: Java Developer (Data Engineering) – Financial Crime Prevention | SEB, Solna
description:
*At SEB, technology is at the heart of everything we do. We are not just supporting the business—we are driving it forward with cutting-edge solutions. Cloud-native platforms and AI-driven innovation, we use a diverse tech stack to solve complex challenges for cloud-native platforms and AI-driven innovation. Join us in shaping the future of financial crime prevention by building reliable services and data pipelines that help protect society. Are you ready for the challenge?*
**Join Our Mission Against Financial Crime**
 Would you like to work with a team of highly skilled Software Engineers to build solutions that empower complex investigations? We’re looking for an experienced
 **Java Developer** 
 with strong
 **Data Engineering** 
 skills to join our Financial Crime Prevention team—where backend engineering, data, and purpose align to detect and prevent bad actors.
   

  

**About The Role**
 As a Java Developer (Data Engineering) in the Financial Crime Prevention team, you will build and maintain Java-based services and data processing capabilities that enable us to detect suspicious behavior and support investigations. You will work end-to-end—from design and implementation to testing, deployment, and operations—in close collaboration with engineers, analysts, and stakeholders in a highly collaborative environment.
   

  

**To thrive in this role, we believe you have:**
* Solid experience with Java (e.g., Java 11+) and building backend services (Spring/Spring Boot or similar)
* Experience building data pipelines (streaming and/or batch) and working with large-scale datasets
* Hands-on with streaming/messaging (e.g., PubSub) and event-driven architectures
* Experience with batch processing frameworks (e.g., Beam) and data modelling
* Familiarity with CI/CD, automated testing, and observability for production systems
* Experience with Infrastructure as Code (e.g., Terraform) and working in cloud environments
* Nice to have: schema registry experience and/or graph databases
* Analytical mindset and ability to solve complex technical problems
* Curiosity and a drive for continuous learning and professional growth
* A collaborative team player who enjoys sharing knowledge and learning from others


**What do we** 
**offer:**
* Meaningful work with real societal impact—helping detect and prevent financial crime
* Opportunity to explore agentic AI in a governed way (LLM-based assistants/agents using tool-calling) to improve engineering workflows and investigation support
* A chance to work with scalable data and integration platforms (streaming and batch) that are critical to SEB’s financial crime prevention capabilities
* A team culture striving for short feedback loops to be more efficient using practices such as CI/CD, automated testing, and continuous improvement
* Flexible work setup: hybrid possibilities and a sustainable work-life balance
* Opportunities to grow through learning, coaching, and working alongside experienced engineers and domain experts


**Do you want to be part of SEB?**
 Welcome to our inclusive culture, where our shared values inspire and uplift our team. We celebrate diversity and strive to ensure every employee feels seen, heard, and valued.
   

  

 Since we select candidates continuously, feel free to send in your application today, but no later than
 **2026-05-11** 
 .
   

  

**Learn more about working at SEB** 
**here** 
 : www.sebgroup.com/techcareers
   

  

 It is our fundamental belief that inclusion and diversity is crucial for our future success. We strive to have an inclusive, value-driven culture where employees feel valued, respected and involved irrespective of who they are, what they believe or where they come from.

title: Category Manager
description:
foodora is part of the Delivery Hero Group, the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience—fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in over 70+ countries worldwide. Headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index.
   

  

**Job Description**
 Do you want to be the superhero of Sweden's grocery retail revolution? Do you have a black (or pink) belt in category management and grocery sales? If you're nodding your head in excitement, keep reading! We're on the hunt for a category mastermind to join our team and help us unleash the awesomeness of Sweden's trendiest digital food chain. At foodora market, we're not just breaking records, we're breaking the mold for the entire grocery industry and now it’s your time to join the ride!
   

  

* Acting as the “business owner” and be fully responsible for the P&L performance of the assigned category
* End to end category management including managing performance of the product portfolio, developing and growing supplier relationships, expanding product selection and supplier base, leading negotiations, setting category strategy, driving sales and margin improvements and creating best-in-class customer experiences
* Ensure foodora market categories has the optimal selection and strategically competitive prices in the market through local and international sourcing
* Source and build strong supplier base to negotiate terms improvement, secure supplier advertising spend, and ensure a reliable and operationally effective flow of goods
* Work to develop new sourcing and extended range to ensure we have high quality, good value goods available to our customers year-round
* Employ data-driven decision making to drive the category. Provide insight to the commercial team and work closely with marketing and supply chain to forecast demand and ensure consistent supply
* Partner with supply chain team to manage inventory levels and wastage
* Work cross functionally with Product, Supply Chain, Operations and Marketing to deliver strategic projects


**Qualifications**
* 3-5 years experience in merchandising / buying, retail and category management
* Experience in supplier negotiations, pricing and promotion, inventory management and product development
* Superb business judgment with a track record of successful negotiations and overall relationship management (both internal and external)
* Ability to work autonomously in a highly demanding and often ambiguous environment, with strong attention to detail and exceptional organizational skills
* Strong analytical skills
* Great communication skills (both oral and written) are a must
* Mastery of MS Office (particularly Excel, Access, and PowerPoint) is preferred
* A plus is if you are highly analytical and strong in following up sales performance, margins, and key commercial KPIs.
* Experience from roles such as controller, strategic purchaser, or similar analytical positions within purchasing or retail is considered a strong advantage, as it brings a structured, data-driven approach to category performance and decision-making.


 Studies have shown that women and some minorities are less likely to apply to jobs unless they meet every qualification. If you are interested in this role and you have most of the skills we’re looking for, we encourage you to apply anyway!
   

  

**Additional Information**
* Wellness allowance topped with the opportunity to participate in many work/life balance initiatives at foodora such as free group training at Sats & reduced price on massage.
* Employee discount at foodora (woho!) and awesome Friday breakfasts!
* Occupational pension, incl. premium exemption insurance, accident insurance and life insurance.
* foodora Learning - Monthly learning sessions about different topics, such as the q-commerce industry, negotiation techniques and project management combined with a structured onboarding and inspiring courses within our learning tool Sana
* Great deals at Benify (for e.g. gym membership, e-bikes hotels, audiobooks, streaming services, clothes, kitchen supplies… and so much more!
* Awesome AW’s and (pink) parties!

title: Customer Support Representative - Sweden
description:
**Mission**
 Neko Health exists to shift healthcare from treating illness to preventing it, using advanced, non‑invasive technology and clinical expertise to deliver early, actionable health insights.
   

  

**Role Purpose**
 Be the first point of contact and a trusted partner to Neko Health's members in the UK. As a Customer Support Representative based in London, you will own multi-channel member support across email, phone, text/SMS, and social — handling everything from booking management and product enquiries to complaint resolution and service recovery. This role is for someone who is empathetic, organised, and calm under pressure, and who takes pride in delivering support that feels genuinely human and member-first at every touchpoint.
   

  

**What You'll Deliver In The First 6–12 Months**
* Every member who contacts Neko feels genuinely looked after — responses are warm, clear, and arrive when they're expected. CSAT scores and SLA adherence reflect this consistently.
* You own each case from first contact to resolution, anticipating what a member needs before they have to ask twice. Repeat contacts fall because members leave interactions feeling truly resolved.
* Whether you're replying to a ticket, handling a complaint, or responding to a comment in public on social, the Neko voice is unmistakable — human, considered, and on brand every time.
* The patterns you notice — recurring frustrations, complaint themes, moments where members stumble — find their way to the right people internally, so the product and experience keep getting better.


**Requirements**
* 2+ years' experience in customer support, hospitality, or another high-touch, member-facing service role
* Fluent in Swedish and English; excellent written and verbal communication in both languages is essential
* Experience supporting customers across multiple channels including phone, email, and digital/social
* Proven ability to manage bookings, troubleshoot issues, and handle complaints with professionalism and accountability
* Familiarity with Zendesk or similar CRM and support platforms
* Strong judgement around tone, timing, and privacy — especially on public-facing social channels
* Highly organised with a clear sense of ownership, strong attention to detail, and ability to manage a personal ticket queue to defined SLAs
* Calm and confident in escalated or high-visibility situations


**Preferred**
* Experience in social or public-facing support is a strong advantage
* Experience with social moderation tools, telephony, and SMS platforms
* Interest in or experience within health, wellness, or technology-driven consumer products


**About Titles At Neko**
 We use a simplified internal title framework that prioritises clarity over hierarchy, so internal titles may differ from market‑facing role titles. Scope, impact and level of the role are fully aligned and will be clearly discussed throughout the process.
   

  

**Hiring Process**
 Candidates progress from application and structured screening through thoughtfully designed interviews culminating in a formal offer and final pre-employment checks before joining the team.
   

  

**Equal Opportunity & Inclusion Statement**
 Neko Health is committed to inclusive hiring and member-first care. We welcome candidates from all backgrounds and encourage you to request reasonable adjustments to support your application.

title: Senior Strategy Specialist
description:
foodora is part of the Delivery Hero Group, the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience—fast, easy, and to your door.
