title: Strategic Purchaser & Sourcing Lead
description:
**Strategic Purchaser & Sourcing Lead** 




 Location: Nyköping / Stockholm
 



 Reporting to: Head of Industrialization & Manufacturing Systems
 



  





**Role Purpose** 




 Own and drive supplier management, material flow, and cost control, ensuring the right materials are available at the right time and cost to support stable and scalable production.
 



  





**Core Mission** 




 Secure a reliable, cost-efficient supply chain that enables production to run without disruption while protecting cash and improving cost structure.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities** 




 Own supplier network across all key components and materials
 



 Re-activate and manage critical suppliers post-bankruptcy
 



 Negotiate pricing, delivery schedules, and payment terms
 



 Ensure material availability aligned with production plan
 



 Maintain rolling 3-month supply visibility (availability + cash impact)
 



 Identify and mitigate supplier and supply chain risks
 



 Identify and qualify secondary suppliers where needed
 



 Ensure BOM cost transparency together with Industrialization
 



 Prevent material bottlenecks through close coordination with production
 



 Own purchasing data, order tracking, and processes in ERP
 



 Drive continuous cost improvements without compromising delivery
 



  





**Non-Negotiables** 




 Strong negotiation and commercial mindset
 



 Clear understanding of cost, cash, and working capital
 



 Structured and disciplined in execution
 



 Takes ownership and drives issues to resolution
 



 Comfortable pushing suppliers and internal stakeholders
 



 Keeps commitments and follows through
 



  





**What Good Looks Like** 




 Materials arrive on time without excess inventory
 



 Suppliers deliver as agreed (price, quality, timing)
 



 Payment terms are improved and respected
 



 No surprises in material availability or cost
 



 Clear visibility of orders, commitments, and risks
 



 Smooth coordination with industrialization and production
 



  





**What Bad Looks Like** 




 Late or missing materials
 



 Poor control of supplier commitments
 



 Cost increases without action
 



 Unclear status of orders or deliveries
 



 Reactive rather than proactive behavior
 



  





  





**Why This Role Matters** 




 This role is critical to ensuring production runs without interruption while maintaining cost control and protecting cash.
 



 The right person ensures that supply chain execution supports both production stability and financial discipline.
 



  





**Key Interfaces** 




 Industrialization – BOM, specifications, cost structure
 



 Production / Site Manager – timing and material needs
 



 Engineering (R&D) – design changes impacting supply
 



 Finance – cash flow and payment strategy
 



  





**Key Principles** 




 Plan ahead – be proactive
 



 Protect cash – timing matters as much as cost
 



 Keep it simple – avoid unnecessary complexity
 



 Be direct – solve issues early
 



 Prioritize what keeps production running

title: QA Engineer
description:
*We're at a pivotal moment for both Tibber and the planet. When joining Tibber, you won’t just help scale a forward-thinking tech company – you’ll contribute to a real shift in how people consume electricity. With millions of smart devices connected to the Tibber platform (like electric vehicles and smart thermostats), we have one of the most unique energy trading portfolios on the market.*
*We’re looking for a QA Engineer to join us in making the energy transition happen – maybe that’s you?*
**Job Mission 🚀**
 As a Quality Engineer at Tibber, you won’t just be finding bugs; you’ll help build the machinery that prevents them. You will play a pivotal role in designing the technical infrastructure and cultural frameworks that allow Tibber to scale without friction.
   

  

 Your goal is to ensure our engineers have the tools, environments, and automated workflows needed to ship world-class software with confidence. You aren't a gatekeeper, you are an accelerator. With a strong focus on the overlap of quality and developer experience, you will help our teams stay productive, even as complexity grows.
   

  

**What You'll Do ✅**
* Strategise & Influence: Work closely with engineering leadership to identify high-impact areas and ensure quality efforts are focused where they matter most.
* Architect Test Environments: Own and evolve our test environments in collaboration with engineering teams, ensuring they are stable, reproducible, and scalable.
* Build the Foundation: Collaborate with the Cloud Platform and DevEx team to build quality support systems that scale across a distributed architecture.
* Full-Stack Automation: Design and maintain automated testing frameworks that support our diverse ecosystem - from high-throughput backends and GraphQL schemas to web frontends and native mobile apps.
* Preach the Quality Gospel: Actively mentor engineers and leaders to instill a "Shift Left" mindset. You’ll help teams establish the standards and metrics needed to own quality throughout the entire development cycle.


**What We're Looking For⚡**
 You are driven by impact and used to fast-paced environments, and you thrive when given clear goals and high accountability. The areas below summarises our ideal candidate:
   

  

* The Polyglot Mindset: You are comfortable jumping into different codebases. Whether it's a microservice in .NET or a mobile component in Swift, you can understand the logic and work with the engineers to find the best ways to test it.
* CI/CD Native: You have deep experience with CI/CD technologies and understand how infrastructure supports (or hinders) quality.
* The Evangelist: You love teaching others and bridging the gap between technical excellence and business impact. You can convince a busy developer that a robust integration test today saves a weekend of on-call duty, but you can just as easily articulate the ROI of quality to a PM or business stakeholder.
* The Self-Starter: You have the autonomy to identify gaps and the initiative to close them. You don't wait for a ticket; you see a bottleneck and you build a bypass.
* The Orchestrator: You are your own best Project Manager. You know how to break down complex architectural goals - like migrating a test suite or spinning up a new staging environment - into actionable milestones. You can manage your own backlog, prioritize ruthlessly based on business risk, and keep stakeholders informed without needing a PM to tail you. You don’t just start projects; you drive them to the finish line.
* The Quality Scientist: You don’t guess; you hypothesise, test, and measure. You are obsessed with observability and data - not just for production uptime, service performance or where we find bugs, but as a "microscope" into the health of our output. You have a natural curiosity for identifying new ways to measure quality and impact, turning "technical hunches" into hard, reproducible data.


**💆🏻‍♀️💆🏽‍♂️The Tibber Mindset**
 Tibber is not your typical energy supplier. Our tech helps hundreds of thousands of households lower and shift their energy consumption to more sustainable and affordable hours. We’re here to accelerate the energy transition – not in theory, but in everyday life.
   

  

 Being on a mission to change an industry, fundamentally also means being prepared for the unexpected. We do what others say can’t be done, try and fail together but never lose sight of our users. We work passionately with sustainability and a circular approach, both with our own products and in the entire ecosystem that is affected by everything we do.
   

  

 Starting out with two passionate founders in 2016, we're now 300+ people working for Tibber in our offices in Stockholm, Førde, Berlin, Amsterdam, and Oslo. We will continue to grow within our markets, and we are thrilled to be backed by investors like Balderton Capital, Eight Roads Ventures, Founders Fund, Summa, and Schibsted.
   

  

*Diversity of thought fuels better products, so we welcome applicants of every background, identity and lived experience. Ready to be part of powering the energy transition?* 
***Apply below!***

title: Security Engineer
description:
At Viaplay Group, we entertain millions of people every day through our streaming services, radio networks, and TV channels. We believe in the power of content not just as a way of telling stories and touching lives, but also expanding worlds.
   

  

 We’re looking for the best people to join us on our journey. Right now, we’re searching for a
 **Security Engineer** 
 in our
 **Stockholm** 
 office – are you ready to hit play on an exciting career change?
   

  

**The Role**
 We are seeking an experienced Security Engineer to join our IT Workplace team, working as a core team member with shared responsibility and close, hands‑on collaboration across all areas of IT. You will support security operations, governance, and compliance within an outsourced, Microsoft‑based hybrid IT environment.
   

  

 The role is responsible for protecting the organisation against cyber threats and embedding security best practices across the Group. You will work closely with colleagues and an outsourced IT partner to deliver effective and pragmatic security outcomes.
   

  

 You will act as a technical lead for security operations, incident response, and security tooling, working collaboratively rather than in isolation, and ensuring security is integrated into day‑to‑day IT operations and change initiatives.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Act as a security lead for IT modernisation and improvement initiatives
* Work with the SOC partner to monitor, investigate, and respond to security alerts and incidents across cloud, on‑premises, and SaaS environments
* Act as the technical escalation point for high‑priority security incidents, coordinating containment, remediation, and recovery activities
* Perform root cause analysis and post‑incident reviews, communicating risks and outcomes to both technical and non‑technical stakeholders.
* Administer and operate security tooling, primarily Microsoft Purview, Defender, and Entra
* Design and manage the implementation of Zero Trust controls utilising Conditional Access, PIM, RBAC, and identity governance
* Maintain and improve security policies, ensuring alignment with recognised standards and regulatory requirements such as ISO 27001 and NIS2
* Support vulnerability management activities, audits, penetration testing, and third‑party risk assessments
* Produce incident, threat, and compliance reporting for stakeholders
* Drive continuous improvement of security controls, tooling, and processes


**What We’re Looking For**
 We understand you may feel confident ticking certain boxes more than others and that’s why we always keep an open mind in our recruitment process. But, in order to thrive in this role, we do believe you’ll have at least some experience in the following:
   

  

* 5+ years’ experience in cybersecurity or security operations roles
* Hands‑on experience with Microsoft Purview, Defender, Entra, and vulnerability and threat management tools.
* Experience working with outsourced IT services and SOC partners
* Microsoft security certifications and other industry‑recognised cybersecurity certifications are highly desirable
* Strong analytical, problem‑solving, and communication skills, with the ability to produce clear technical and non‑technical documentation
* Relevant education in IT, cybersecurity, or a related discipline, or equivalent professional experience


 What we offer you
   

  

* We’ve got you covered! 30 days of paid vacation every year, an attractive pension and insurance scheme, and generous parental leave pay lift.
* Your wellbeing matters. We provide you with various wellbeing initiatives including wellness allowance.
* A safe space to grow and up-skill. Our learning culture puts you in the driver’s seat of your own development.
* An innovative environment with Hack Days once a year. This week-long initiative allows you to think outside the box and deliver creative, technical solutions that (more often than not) go on to be implemented, either in our product or our ways of working.
* Entertainment is what we love, and entertainment is what we do. So, unlimited access to Viaplay seems only fair for you to get to know the product – including serier & viewing events, new release movie rentals, linear channels and more.


**Hit play today**
 If this feels like your kind of challenge, make sure you apply by attaching your CV here – you may also want to add your LinkedIn profile. Please don’t send us your application via email because we won’t be able to accept it. We do, however, welcome any questions you may have about this particular position.
   

  

 Want to learn more about who we are and what we do? Check out our careers page or follow us on Instagram! We’re only ever a few clicks away.

title: Engineering Manager - Subscriptions
description:
The Subscriptions team is part of Spotify’s broader mission to build seamless, scalable experiences that support how users access and pay for Spotify. This includes enabling sustainable growth through reliable, flexible, and innovative subscription and commerce systems.
   

  

 Spotify's Subscriptions Lifecycle Engine (SLE) powers the commerce systems behind every subscription product we offer. From Premium plans to add-ons and new tiers, over 90% of Spotify's revenue flows through this area. You’ll lead teams working at the heart of Spotify’s business, shaping systems that directly impact millions of users and our global revenue engine.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

* Lead, support, and grow a team of engineers at different stages of their careers, creating an environment where people can do their best work
* Own delivery and technical direction for 1–2 squads focused on subscription management, order processing, or third-party integrations
* Partner closely with Product Managers to shape priorities and translate business goals into clear, actionable engineering plans
* Ensure reliability and operational excellence for high-scale, revenue-critical systems, including on-call practices and incident response
* Collaborate with other Engineering Managers across SLE to align on ways of working, technical standards, and team health
* Navigate compliance requirements such as SOX controls and financial reporting accuracy
* Contribute to the evolution of Spotify’s commerce platform as we scale our subscriber base and revenue


 Who You Are
   

  

* You have experience leading software engineering teams, including hiring, coaching, and supporting performance and growth
* You are experienced with backend or distributed systems and can contribute to technical discussions and architectural decisions
* You understand how to operate in complex, high-impact domains where changes can affect revenue and financial reporting
* You are comfortable balancing multiple priorities and working with a range of stakeholders
* You collaborate effectively in distributed teams and across time zones
* You care about building inclusive teams and creating an environment where people feel supported and empowered
* You have worked on e-commerce, payments, or subscription systems, or are excited to learn in this space


 Where You'll Be
   

  

* This role is based in London or Stockholm
* We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home


 Spotify is an equal opportunity employer. You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be forward-thinking! So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.
   

  

 At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. We have ways to request reasonable accommodations during the interview process and help assist in what you need. If you need accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know - we’re here to support you in any way we can.
   

  

 Spotify transformed music listening forever when we launched in 2008. Our mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the chance to enjoy and be passionate about these creators. Everything we do is driven by our love for music and podcasting. Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service.

title: Senior systemutvecklare
description:
Vi söker en
 **senior systemutvecklare (kompetensnivå 4)** 
 för ett långsiktigt uppdrag inom systemutveckling och systemförvaltning av egenutvecklade system med fokus på
 **finansiella lösningar** 
 .
 



  





 Uppdraget omfattar vidareutveckling och förvaltning av verksamhetskritiska system som används för budgetering, redovisning och uppföljning. Arbetet bedrivs i
 **Microsoftmiljö** 
 med ett
 **agilt arbetssätt** 
 , där kvalitet, stabila leveranser och nära samarbete med beställare står i centrum.
 



  





 Om uppdraget
 



 Du kommer att arbeta i ett av flera agila utvecklingsteam tillsammans med systemutvecklare, teamledare, testare och kravanalytiker. Totalt omfattar organisationen cirka 30 personer fördelade på tre team. Arbetet sker i nära dialog med beställare, vilket ställer höga krav på samarbetsförmåga, arkitekturförståelse och verksamhetsnära systemutveckling.
 



 Verksamheten verkar inom statlig sektor, vilket innebär att förståelse för statliga processer och regelverk är en viktig del av uppdraget. Erfarenhet av finansiella system inom exempelvis ekonomi, redovisning, budget eller revision är därför starkt meriterande.
 



  





 Ska‑krav
 


* Kompetensnivå 4 (motsvarande 9–12 års erfarenhet)
* Minst 9 års sammanlagd erfarenhet (de senaste 12 åren) av avancerad systemutveckling
* Minst 9 års erfarenhet (de senaste 12 åren) av webbutveckling i
 **Microsoftmiljö**
* Minst 9 års erfarenhet (de senaste 12 åren) av
 **agilt arbetssätt** 
 och av att arbeta nära både utvecklingsteam och beställare
* Minst 2 års erfarenhet (de senaste 4 åren) av att arbeta verksamhetsnära med att strukturera och designa IT‑lösningar utifrån verksamhetsbehov och befintlig IT‑miljö
* Minst 2 års erfarenhet (de senaste 4 åren) av uppdrag inom
 **statlig verksamhet**
* Minst 2 års erfarenhet (de senaste 4 åren) av utveckling av
 **avancerade finansiella system** 
 med komplex funktionalitet och affärslogik
* Mycket goda kunskaper i
 **svenska** 
 , både i tal och skrift



  





 Referensuppdrag
 



 Skriftlig referens på relevant uppdrag ska kunna lämnas senast dagen efter intervju, vid förfrågan.
 



  





 Praktisk information
 


* **Omfattning:** 
 Heltid (100 %)
* **Arbetsort:** 
 Stockholm
* **Distansarbete:** 
 Möjligt upp till 50 %
* **Period:** 
 2026‑09‑01 – 2028‑08‑30
* **Option:** 
 Förlängning upp till 2 år
* **Intervjuer:** 
 Digital intervju planeras till 11/5 eller 13/5 2026

title: Manager/Senior Manager, Data & Analytics
description:
**Manager/Senior Manager, Data & Analytics** 




  





 At Capgemini Invent, we believe difference drives change. As inventive transformation consultants, we blend our strategic, creative and scientific capabilities, collaborating closely with clients to deliver cutting-edge solutions. Join us to drive transformation tailored to our client's challenges of today and tomorrow. Informed and validated by science and data. Superpowered by creativity and design. All underpinned by technology created with purpose.
 



  





**Your Role** 




 As a Manager/Senior Manager in Enterprise Data & Analytics, you will lead clients through data‑driven transformation, helping them build strong data foundations, modern analytics capabilities, and AI‑enabled ways of working. You will guide the full journey from strategy to execution, translating data into insights and insights into measurable business value. In addition, you will contribute to sales and business development by supporting opportunity identification, proposal development, and relationship‑building activities that help drive the continued growth of our practice. For this role you will play a key role in:
 


* Leading the development of enterprise data strategies, governance frameworks, and data‑culture initiatives.
* Overseeing the implementation of modern data platforms that provide trusted, high‑quality data foundations.
* Advising senior stakeholders on AI‑powered innovation to enable faster, more informed decision‑making.
* Guiding end‑to‑end data‑journey transformation using proven frameworks and methodologies.
* Leading multidisciplinary teams delivering enterprise‑scale analytics, insights, and business value.
* Contributing to sales and business development activities, including supporting opportunity identification, proposal development, and client‑relationship building.



  





**Your Profile** 



* Management consulting experience within Data & AI transformation.
* Broad expertise across the Data & AI landscape, including modern data platforms (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks), data strategy and governance, AI use‑case development, and AI architecture; and the ability to serve as the bridge between business needs and technical solutions.
* Bringing strong communication and stakeholder management capabilities, with the ability to contribute to business development efforts in line with the expectations of the seniority of the role.
* A master’s degree with a business, technical or information technology or data & analytics focus
* Excellent Swedish and English language skills and Swedish work permit required.



  





**What You’ll Love About Working Here** 



* Impactful Work: Help organizations unlock the full potential of data and AI to drive operational excellence, sustainability, and strategic decision-making. You’ll enable clients to become truly data-driven by defining strategies, governance frameworks, and execution plans that transform vision into reality.
* Innovation at Scale: Work at the intersection of business, strategy, data, and AI, leveraging cutting-edge technologies and methodologies to create intelligent products, operations, and services.
* Career Growth: Access world-class training, mentorship, and opportunities to deepen your expertise in AI, Data Governance, and advanced analytics—accelerating your professional journey.
* Collaborative Culture: Join a diverse, inclusive team that values curiosity, creativity, and bold ideas. Together, we explore, experiment, and execute strategies that make a real difference.
* Purpose-Driven Projects: Help clients harness data and AI to achieve strategic objectives, increase business value, and build sustainable, customer-centric solutions that shape the future



  





**Need To Know (Local Section)** 




 In line with our trust-based culture, Capgemini Invent offers a hybrid working model, and for this role you will be based in our attractively located Stockholm office, ready to travel to our clients’ sites when needed.
 



  





**As part of our recruitment process, identity verification will be conducted through inspection of your ID card, and background checks may be carried out as required.** 




  





 Sounds like the next step in your career? We look forward to receiving your application and learning more about your experience and aspirations.
 



  





**About Capgemini** 




 Capgemini is a global business and technology transformation partner, helping organizations to accelerate their dual transition to a digital and sustainable world, while creating tangible impact for enterprises and society. It is a responsible and diverse group of 340,000 team members in more than 50 countries. With its strong over 55-year heritage, Capgemini is trusted by its clients to unlock the value of technology to address the entire breadth of their business needs. It delivers end-to-end services and solutions leveraging strengths from strategy and design to engineering, all fueled by its market leading capabilities in AI, generative AI, cloud and data, combined with its deep industry.
 



  





 #LI-DNI

title: Applied AI Engineer – Production
description:
Are you passionate about turning data into impactful solutions? Do you want to work hands-on with advanced AI/ML models in a high-tech manufacturing environment? At Silex, you will play a key role in transforming MEMS manufacturing through intelligent, data-driven solutions — improving quality, efficiency, and delivery performance. This is an opportunity to take ownership of end-to-end AI/ML development and make a real difference in a cutting-edge MEMS environment.
   

  

 Would you like to know more about Silex and what we do? Read more here.
   

  

**Team Production**
 You will be part of our Production team, where the main focus is to achieve our On-Time Delivery goals and make our process more efficient every day. The team consists of highly skilled and collaborative people focused on enabling smarter manufacturing and works cross-functionally with our process engineers, engineering, IT, and quality to improve processes, enhance yield, and support innovation. We are now expanding our Production team with two AI Applied Engineers to implement AI/ML to our manufacturing facility. In this role you will be part of shaping the future of AI-driven manufacturing at Silex.
   

  

**In this role, you will:**
* Drive AI/ML strategy and roadmap, including architecture, tools, and governance practices
* Develop and deploy end-to-end AI/ML solutions, from proof-of-concept to fully productionized systems
* Build and maintain robust pipelines for inference, monitoring, retraining, and version control
* Monitor, validate, and continuously improve deployed models in production environments
* Perform data-driven root cause analysis to identify and resolve process and quality issues
* Analyze production and equipment data to detect patterns and mitigate risks
* Support the organization with advanced data modeling and analytics for better decision-making


**We are looking for someone who has:**
* A Master’s or PhD in material science, physics, chemistry, data science, or a related field
* Experience in end-to-end AI & ML development, including deployment and monitoring in a production environment
* Programming skills in Python and experience with libraries such as scikit-learn, pandas, and NumPy
* Experience with experiment tracking and model management tools (e.g., MLflow or similar)
* Solid understanding of statistical analysis and ability to communicate insights clearly
* Familiarity with version control (Git) and deployment practices such as Docker and REST APIs
* Strong communication skills in English; Swedish is considered an advantage


**What we offer you:**
* The chance to work closely with fantastic colleagues.
* The opportunity to work with the latest technology in MEMS.
* To be part of a leading company driving technology development in the industry.
* Strong knowledge exchange with the world's foremost experts in process integration and process development of MEMS.


**About Silex**
 Silex is the world’s largest and most advanced pure-play MEMS foundry, headquartered in Stockholm. MEMS is present in nearly all modern technology, from personal gadgets to automotive electronics, medical monitoring and testing equipment, and thermal management systems. With a team of expert engineers, operators, technicians, support staff, specialists, and more, Silex brings the ideas and concepts of the industry’s leading MEMS innovators to life.
   

  

 The Silex team brings together talented, curious, visionary, and determined individuals to break new ground in the industry alongside customers, developing the latest process technology within MEMS for global applications. As part of this team, you will work with some of the world’s largest technology giants and innovation leaders in industries such as medicine, life sciences, consumer electronics, telecommunications, automotive, and industrial sectors.
   

  

**Additional Information**
 We have an ongoing recruitment process and therefore encourage you to submit your application as soon as possible. To apply, please attach your CV and answer a few questions. Our process is competency-based and includes a personality and logic test as well as reference checks. Additionally, we will conduct a background check and drug test prior to employment, and as an employer, we perform random alcohol and drug tests for all employees.
   

  

 We look forward to your application and to welcoming you on this exciting journey!
   

  

**Silex** 
 is the world’s largest and most advanced pure-play MEMS foundry. With a team of expert engineers, operators, technicians, engineers, support staff, specialists, and much more besides, Silex transforms the visionary ideas of the world’s leading MEMS innovators into reality.

title: Security Architect
description:
**Job Description**
 DRAFT, to be reviewed before posting.
   

  

 We are looking for a
 **pragmatic and experienced Security Architect** 
 to join our organization. This role is not about writing policies for the sake of policies – it is about
 **designing security that works in real environments** 
 .
   

  

 You will support our customers and internal teams by translating security, regulatory, and business requirements into
 **practical, sustainable, and implementable security solutions** 
 . You will be involved early in customer engagements and play a key role in ensuring
 **Security by Design** 
 across architectures, platforms, and services.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
 As a Security Architect with us, you will work on several levels:
   

  

* Support new customer engagements by understanding security, regulatory, and business requirements and translating them into concrete security architectures and solutions
* Design security as an integrated part of IT solutions – Security by Design, not bolted‑on controls
* Advise architects, delivery teams, and customers on security‑related decisions across infrastructure, applications, services and cloud
* Perform practical assessments of solutions and environments against relevant industry standards and frameworks
* Support internal improvement initiatives related to security architecture, governance, and compliance
* Act as a bridge between technical teams, security requirements, and business needs


 The focus should always be on
 **outcome and usability** 
 , not theoretical perfection.
   

  

**Who We Are Looking For**
**As our future** 
**Information Security Manager** 
**, you will:**
 You are a
 **hands‑on security architect** 
 who enjoys solving real problems and working close to both customers and delivery teams.
   

  

**We Believe You Have**
* Several years of experience working with security architecture in real delivery environments
* Strong ability to translate requirements into solutions, not just documents
* A practical understanding of Security by Design principles
* Experience working with standards and frameworks such as ISO 27001, NIST, NIS2, CIS Controls, or similar
* Experience engaging with customers, understanding their needs, and advising on security matters
* The confidence to challenge solutions when security is not good enough – and propose better alternatives


**Your profile**
 You are proactive, structured, and analytical, with a strong sense of responsibility.
   

  

 This role is intended for a
 **Security Architect** 
 who has reached their level through
 **several years of hands‑on experience** 
 in security architecture roles.
   

  

**We Are Looking For Someone Who Is**
* Strongly solution‑oriented – you focus on how security is actually implemented and works in practice
* Experienced in making architectural security decisions in real customer and delivery environments
* Comfortable taking ownership and responsibility for security outcomes, not just recommendations
* Used to balancing security requirements, technical constraints, and business needs


 You have built your seniority by
 **designing, assessing, and improving real security solutions** 
 , and you are confident working independently while collaborating closely with architects, engineers, technicians, project managers and customers.
   

  

 You have experience working closely with customers in complex IT and operational environments and the ability to work in a structured, independent, and advisory manner. You also have excellent communication skills in both Swedish and English, spoken and written, and you have documented experience in leading security work at an account level or equivalent.
   

  

**What is in it for you?**
* A key role with real influence over how security is designed and implemented
* The opportunity to work close to customers and technical teams
* Complex and meaningful security challenges in modern IT environments
* A professional, collaborative culture with a strong focus on delivery and trust


 The role requires security clearance, background checks, and Swedish citizenship.

title: PIM Coordinator
description:
**At Foxway, we enable sustainable tech for everyone.**
 We do this by working sustainably in all dimensions – environmentally, socially, and economically. We are Service Thinkers and strive to create win-win outcomes for our customers, our business, and the environment.
   

  

 Now, we’re looking for a
 **PIM (Product Information Management) Coordinator** 
 early in their career to join our Deal Desk team
 **.** 
 In this role, you’ll take ownership of product master data and help ensure our customer-facing systems are accurate, structured, and up to date.
   

  

****What you'll be doing****
* Responsible for product master data across systems ERP and Foxway Digital Platform.
* Maintain data quality, structures and standards.
* Responsible for product creation, updates, and lifecycle management.
* Support daily work related to products, services, and bundles.
* Ensure compliance with internal processes, sustainability goals, and quality standards.
* You will work closely with Deal Desk, Sales & Customer Success, and IT department.


****Who we are looking for****
* You are early in your career (0–3 years of experience) and eager to grow within IT-enabled business processes like handling master data, ERP-systems and digital product descriptions and specifications.
* Ability to work both operationally and structurally.
* Fluency in Swedish and English, both written and spoken.
* Bachelor degree in Engineering, Finance, Business or Supply Chain Management, or other Education relevant to excel at Foxway.
* Based and are able to work in our HQ in Stockholm (Solna).


**Personal Qualities**
* Structured and detail-oriented.
* Proactive and accountable.
* Collaborative and service-minded.


****Why join Foxway?****
* Be part of a fast-growing circular tech company
* Work close to the commercial core of the business
* Collaborate across countries, functions, and systems
* Contribute directly to sustainability and circular economy


****Sounds like your next step?****
 Send in your application! We review candidates on an ongoing basis, so don’t wait too long 🚀
   

  

 #Foxway

title: Information Architect
description:
**I** 
**nformationsarkitekter till B3** 
**Visab Consulting**
 Hos B3 Visab Consulting stödjer vi finansindustrins utveckling i den digitala ekonomin. Vi söker nu fler engagerade informationsarkitekter som vill vara med och bidra till en samhällskritisk bransch. Med över 40 års erfarenhet har vi byggt långsiktiga relationer med ledande aktörer inom bank och försäkring, och vår expertis gör oss även till en attraktiv partner för snabbväxande företag.
   

  

**Om rollen** 
 Som informationsarkitekt hos oss får du arbeta i varierande uppdrag där du hjälper kunder att utveckla och implementera robust informationsarkitektur. Du kommer att:
   

  

* Skapa riktlinjer och standarder för informationsarkitekturens struktur.
* Sätta upp processer för att hantera och vidareutveckla informationsflöden.
* Säkerställa att rätt information når rätt personer vid rätt tidpunkt.
* Arbeta med datalagring, tillgänglighet och säkerhet för att förhindra obehörig åtkomst och dataförluster.


 Vi tror att framgång bygger på samarbete och kunskapsdelning och vi ser gärna att du är en lagspelare som uppskattar att arbeta prestigelöst och flexibelt.
   

  

**Vi söker dig som har:**
* Minst 3 års erfarenhet som informationsarkitekt eller enterprisearkitekt, med fokus på informationsstruktur.
* Minst 2 års erfarenhet från bank- eller försäkringssektorn.
* Tidigare erfarenhet som konsult.
* Goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska, både i tal och skrift.
* Certifieringar inom relevanta områden är meriterande, liksom erfarenhet som verksamhetsarkitekt.


**Vi erbjuder:**
* Attraktiv och flexibel lönemodell.
* Möjlighet att påverka dina arbetstider och arbetsplats.
* Tillgång till utbildningar och certifieringar för din professionella utveckling.
* En stark wellness-satsning genom Team B3, som främjar både hälsa och arbetsglädje.


**Varför B3** 
 Visab? Vi är en del av B3 Consulting Group, ett av Sveriges snabbast växande företag inom IT och management. Hos oss får du det bästa av två världar: kraften från ett stort bolag och den personliga samhörigheten från ett mindre. Vi är stolta över att ha blivit erkända som "Årets Karriärföretag" och av "Great Place to Work". Våra medarbetare pratar om friheten att påverka sina uppdrag, arbetsmiljön där olikheter välkomnas, och stoltheten över att bidra till både kunders framgång och samhället i stort. Vi samarbetar med initiativ som Women in Tech, Changers Hub och Pink Programming för att göra skillnad.
   

  

**Kontakt** 
 :
   

  

 Är du redo att ta nästa steg i din karriär? Ansök nu! Har du frågor är du välkommen att kontakta Lidia Hagos (lidia.hagos@b3.se) eller konsultchef Hanna Skoog (+4670-260 12 65).

title: Marketing Associate EMEA
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is on a mission: to redefine how legal work gets done. From the very start we have been very clear about the fact that we are not building a solution for lawyers, we are building it with them, because it is the only way to make sure it gets done the right way; working side-by-side every step of the way.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace empowers legal professionals not just to work faster - but to ask better questions, unlock new insights. Every day, we push the boundaries of legal tech to make complex processes smarter, faster, and more human. From thousands of documents analysed in minutes to intelligent workflows designed in collaboration with leading practices, we’re turning possibility into reality.
   

  

 Today we are trusted by global firms like Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Bird & Bird and Linklaters in over 40 countries, but we have no plans on stopping here. We ship fast, we iterate effectively, and we scale rapidly - not by accident, but by design.
   

  

 When you join Legora, you become part of a team that believes "good enough" isn’t good enough and that the way to win is together, by empowering lawyers to do their best work with technology that truly understands them. If you’re excited by building from first principles, working with exceptional people, and accelerating change in a high-stakes, high-impact domain—then this is the moment and the place.
   

  

**We’re not just shaping the future of legal tech — we’re defining it. Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
 We’re looking for a Marketing Associate, EMEA to support the execution of high-impact marketing programs across the region. This is an exciting opportunity for someone early in their marketing career who wants hands-on experience building a category-defining brand inside a fast-scaling SaaS company.
   

  

 You’ll work closely with the Marketing Director EMEA and collaborate with global teams across Growth, Demand Generation, Product Marketing and Brand. You’ll be embedded in regional go-to-market initiatives, helping bring campaigns and experiences to life that drive brand awareness, pipeline growth, and customer engagement.
   

  

 This role is execution-focused and highly cross-functional. It’s ideal for someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment, enjoys managing details, and takes pride in delivering high-quality work.
   

  

 What you’ll do
   

  

* Support the execution of regional marketing campaigns across EMEA, ensuring strong brand consistency and operational excellence.
* Assist in planning and delivering industry conferences, executive exchanges, community events, and Legora-hosted experiences.
* Coordinate event logistics end-to-end, including vendor management, attendee communications, run-of-show planning, and on-site execution.
* Partner with Growth and Demand Generation teams to support account-based initiatives and regional field marketing programs.
* Help develop and amplify regional content, including case studies, announcements, customer stories, and event follow-ups.
* Support campaign activation across email, social, paid media, and digital channels.
* Track and report on performance metrics, contributing insights to improve future programs.
* Manage relationships with regional vendors and external partners to ensure seamless delivery and budget alignment.
* Contribute creative ideas that elevate Legora’s brand presence across every touchpoint.


 What you bring
   

  

* 2–4 years of experience in B2B marketing, events, communications, or a related field.
* Hands-on experience supporting events, sponsorships, campaigns, or community initiatives.
* Exceptional organizational skills and attention to detail — you’re comfortable managing multiple moving parts simultaneously.
* Strong written and verbal communication skills in English.
* A proactive, solution-oriented mindset and a desire to learn and grow quickly.
* Comfort collaborating across teams and regions in a dynamic, fast-moving environment.
* Experience with CRM and marketing tools (e.g., Salesforce, HubSpot, Pardot, Customer.io) is a plus.
* Language skills in German, Spanish, and/or French are a plus.


 What we offer
   

  

* The opportunity to contribute to one of the fastest-growing SaaS brands in legal technology.
* Direct exposure to senior stakeholders and cross-functional leadership.
* Accelerated learning and career growth in an ambitious, scaling environment.
* Competitive salary and equity participation.


 If you’re excited about building meaningful experiences, supporting high-impact programs, and shaping the future of legal AI across EMEA, apply.
   

  

**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Test Engineer
description:
**Where Testing Meets Technology—Join Alfa Laval’s R&D Team**
**Alfa Laval is a global leader in engineering innovation and sustainability. We’re looking for a passionate Test Engineer to help advance our HSS (High Speed Separation) product portfolio, from concept to reality and beyond. If you thrive on hands-on engineering and want to make a real impact, this is your opportunity to shape the future of technology!**
 As a Test Engineer, you will be part of a collaborative team of 8 Test Engineers, working closely together on projects and sharing knowledge. You will play a pivotal role in product development projects and R&D activities, executing development and verification testing to ensure our products meet rigorous standards and regulatory requirements. You will work across all Alfa Laval divisions—Ocean, Food & Pharma, and Energy—giving you broad exposure and impact.
   

  

 Most of your work will be based in our lab in Flemingsberg, but you will also have opportunities to work in the field. You’ll be close to the product, seeing the direct impact of your work, providing feedback to designers, and driving real improvements. Your hands-on testing and reporting will influence design reviews and project decisions, making your role highly visible and impactful.
   

  

**Your Responsibilities**
* Responsible for execution of development, and verification testing as part of product development projects and other R&D activities.
* Create test plans based on product specifications regulatory requirements, outlining test objectives, methods, schedules and acceptance criteria.
* Lead installation meetings with support function within the innovation lab
* Define test setups by selecting appropriate tools, equipment configuration in collaboration with project team.
* Prepare test object for testing by performing service and mechanical assembly according to manuals or instructions.
* Evaluate test results through data analysis and mechanical inspection, identify deviations from expected performance, and collaborate with cross-functional teams to investigate root causes and implement corrective actions.
* Produce test Report that document procedures, result, deviations and conclusions supporting design reviews and project decisions.
* Participate in development of new technical solutions and testing methods, in close collaboration with other parts of the R&D organization


**Your Skills**
* Mechanical background or similar
* Experience in product testing, development, or R&D; experience in testing at other companies is a plus
* Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
* Proficiency in data analysis and mechanical inspection
* Excellent communication and teamwork abilities
* Open, forward-thinking, proactive, and eager to learn new things and technologies
* Commitment to safety and quality standards
* Good command of both English and Swedish, spoken and written


**What’s in it for you?**
 A challenging position in an international, open, and friendly environment where we help each other grow. You value being part of a larger organization that develops important solutions, while occasionally having the opportunity to be more creative.
   

  

 You’ll work in a brand-new office in Flemingsberg, easily accessible by public transport, with parking facilities and a modern bicycle garage. Our laboratory is among the most modern in Stockholm!
   

  

**For more information, please contact**
 Gijs van der Post, Talent Acquisition Partner, gijs.vanderpost@alfalaval.com
   

  

 Einar Hallgren, Manager Testing Technology, einar.hallgren@alfalaval.com
   

  

**Union information**
 Anders Jansson, Unionen, Anders.Jansson@alfalaval.com
   

  

 Axel Scharing, Akademikerna, Axel.Scharing@alfalaval.com
   

  

**Alcohol and drug testing**
 As part of Alfa Laval’s commitment to a safe and healthy workplace, alcohol and drug testing is applied during the pre‑employment process.
   

  

**Medical check-up**
 This position is subject to mandatory medical examinations in accordance with AFS 2023:15.
   

  

*We care about diversity, inclusion, and equity in our recruitment processes. We also believe behavioral traits can provide important insights into a candidate's fit to a role. To help us achieve this we apply Pymetrics assessments, and upon application you will be invited to play the assessment games.  Playing games is mandatory.*
*Please submit your application via our website. Applications by e-mail will not be considered.*

title: Senior Backend Engineer
description:
**About Lifesum**
 Used by over 65 million people globally, Lifesum is an AI-powered nutrition platform that helps people make smarter food choices and build sustainable, long-term habits. From improving focus and energy to managing weight and overall wellbeing, Lifesum provides personalized meal plans, recipes, and tracking tools that fit every lifestyle. Learn more at www.lifesum.com
   

  

 We’re looking for a
 **Senior Backend Engineer** 
 to help design, build, and scale the services that power our platform.
   

  

**Note: This role is on-site.** 
 You’ll be based at our Stockholm headquarters at Repslagargatan 17, Södermalm, working in a hybrid setup.
   

  

**What You'll Do**
* Design and ship backend services on our Go, Postgres, and Kinesis stack.
* Work across a broad surface area, from high-throughput ingestion pipelines to payment and entitlement systems to AI-backed personalisation.
* Partner day-to-day with product managers, designers, mobile engineers, and data to turn fuzzy problems into well-scoped services, and make sure what you ship actually lands with users.
* Use AI coding agents as a first-class part of your workflow, champion improvements to our internal skills library, and help raise the bar for how the team works with them day to day.
* Own features end-to-end from RFC through production, including the observability, alerting, and documentation that come with a grown-up service.
* Mentor peers, push back on weak design, and help shape our tech radar.


****Who you are****
 You've operated at senior level in a high-growth environment and care about craft as much as velocity. You're a generous collaborator - the kind of engineer people want in the room when things get hard - and you communicate clearly in writing and in person. You're comfortable pushing back, comfortable being wrong, and comfortable shipping.
   

  

* Expert at building backend systems in Go, ideally in a microservice or modular-monolith context. Comfortable reaching for another language when the problem calls for it (we have some legacy Python hanging around).
* Strong with event-driven architectures. We use Kinesis heavily; prior exposure to event sourcing & CQRS is a real plus.
* Comfortable on AWS, with Kubernetes (we run EKS) and infrastructure as code.
* You use AI coding agents in your daily work - we’ve standardised on Claude Code - and can speak to how you structure context, critique outputs, and keep the feedback loop tight. The use of AI assistants is not optional.
* Strong communicator across disciplines. You can write a clear RFC, argue its merits with engineers and non-engineers alike, and commit gracefully if a different direction is chosen.
* Genuinely curious about the problem, not just the code. You want to know why a feature matters to users, not just what to build.


**Nice to have**
* Experience with payments, subscriptions, or entitlement platforms.
* Background in a regulated domain - health (GDPR Article 9), finance (PCI), or similar.
* Distributed tracing and observability experience.
* Scale-up or startup experience - you know what "good enough" looks like at different stages.


****Life at Lifesum****
 You’ll be part of a purpose-led company redefining the future of digital health and nutrition. We move fast, work together, and create space for your ideas to thrive — with real impact, every single day. And because health and wellbeing are our mission, we live it too.
   

  

 At Lifesum, you'll find:
   

  

* Hybrid work setup with our HQ in Stockholm
* Fast decision-making and real ownership — less bureaucracy, more impact
* A culture of curiosity — collaboration and engagement
* Shared wellbeing experiences — cook, train, and prioritize your health together
* Comprehensive benefits — private health insurance, personal development budget, generous wellness allowance, and more


**Base salary range:** 
 TBD (Depending on experience and role scope)
   

  

**Start date:** 
 As soon as possible.
   

  

 We review applications on an ongoing basis — don’t sleep on it!
   

  

*Please note that we kindly decline inquiries from external recruitment agencies.*

title: Project Manager
description:
***This is us***
 At Avenga, we believe that human creativity empowers technology that matters. Operating globally, our 6000+ specialists provide a full spectrum of services, including business and tech advisory, enterprise solutions, CX, UX and Ul design, managed services, product development, and software development.
   

  

***This is the job***
 Avenga is looking for a skilled
 **Project Manager / Change Lead** 
 to join our team full-time. You will be supporting large-scale IT transformation programs across major organizations in Sweden.
   

  

 As a Senior Change Lead, you will drive and coordinate change management activities within complex IT programs, working closely with program management, project managers, operations, and IT teams. Your role will be critical in ensuring successful adoption of new systems, processes, and ways of working.
   

  

 You will act as a bridge between strategic transformation goals and operational execution, ensuring that change initiatives are effectively implemented, communicated, and embedded across the organization.
   

  

***This is you***
 :
   

  

* Senior Change Lead with experience of the role from IT transformation programs in larger organizations
* Experience of change management in connection with IT transformation programs and system changes
* Experience and knowledge of established frameworks, methods and tools in change management, such as Prosci ADKAR and similar
* Experience in coordinating change management with communication and training efforts, as well as following up on implementation, risks and readiness
* Good understanding of IT projects and project processes as well as knowledge of integrating Change Management and IT project planning
* Pragmatic approach and can oscillate between a holistic perspective and operational execution, such as facilitation, analysis and production of documentation
* Excellent communication and presentation skills in Swedish and English


***This is your role***
* Lead and drive change management activities within major IT programs and transformations
* Collaborate closely with program management, project managers, IT, and business stakeholders
* Align change initiatives with communication and training strategies
* Assess organizational readiness, identify risks, and implement mitigation plans
* Ensure effective integration of change management into IT project planning and execution
* Facilitate workshops, stakeholder alignment sessions, and change impact analyses
* Develop and maintain change documentation, plans, and reporting structures
* Monitor adoption and ensure sustainable implementation of changes
* Act as a key advisor on change strategy and execution across the organization


*At Avenga, everyone matters. We provide equal opportunities in recruitment, career development, and leadership, regardless of race, ethnicity, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, age, religion, or any other characteristic. We are committed to fostering a work environment where our diverse community of employees, candidates, and business partners actively shapes our growth. By bringing together people from different backgrounds and experiences, we build a workplace where everyone feels free to be themselves while honoring the boundaries of others.*

title: Copywriter
description:
**WE ARE LOOKING FOR** 




 A creative and customer-focused Copywriter with a strong passion for fashion and storytelling. You have a deep understanding of tone of voice and know how to create engaging, relevant content across multiple digital channels. You thrive in a fast-paced environment, take initiative, and enjoy collaborating with others to deliver impactful communication that strengthens the brand and drives customer engagement.
 



  





**This is a temporary assignment from end of May and 6 months ahead** 




  





**RESPONSIBILITIES AND TASKS** 



* Develop copy for digital channels across fashion activities and campaigns, aligned with project plans, tone of voice, and brand direction
* Create versatile, customer-focused content for hm.com (web and app), email, push notifications, and social media
* Conduct research based on purpose, product, and target audience to generate strong copy ideas for each project
* Adapt messaging across channels while maintaining consistency in tone and brand identity
* Stay up to date on customer behaviour, trends, and communication best practices
* Ensure all copy is legally compliant from a copyright perspective



  





**QUALIFICATIONS** 



* **Must:** 
 +3 years of experience in English copywriting within fashion, lifestyle, or retail
* Strong interest in fashion and current trends
* Solid understanding of brand tone of voice and storytelling
* Proven ability to create engaging, customer-centric content tailored for multiple channels
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English



  





**You are also:** 



* A clear and confident communicator
* Driven, proactive, and able to stay organized in a fast-paced environment
* Curious, collaborative, and eager to learn and grow in a dynamic setting



  





**ABOUT THE COMPANY** 




 H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make. We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future.
 



  





**OTHER INFORMATION** 




 This is a temporary assignment from
 **end of May and 6 months ahead** 
 The role is based on-site at H&M headquarters centrally located office in Stockholm. We are reviewing applications on a rolling basis to find the right candidate as soon as possible, so
 **please apply today!** 




  





*For WorkStudio it is important to offer equal opportunities. We will therefore consider all qualified applications for employment without any regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, or age.*

title: Planned Communications Producer
description:
**Job Description**

 We’re looking for Planned Communications Producers to join our Global CRM team at H&M and play a key role in bringing our customer communications to life. In this role, you’ll build, configure and deliver planned communications across our owned channels; Email, Push Notifications, SMS and WhatsApp, ensuring every campaign is executed with precision, pace, and visual coherence.
   

  





 You’ll combine strong operational discipline together with a collaborative mindset - working closely with regions and stakeholders to deliver high-quality campaigns. You’ll continuously optimize performance and support a seamless, relevant customer experience across all touchpoints.
   

  





**WHAT YOU’LL DO**




 Planned Communications Production
   

  




* Build, configure and deploy planned communications across owned channels in line with approved briefs, templates and global standards.
* Set up campaigns in the marketing automation platform with speed and accuracy, applying predefined segmentation, personalization and sequencing rules to ensure high-quality execution.
* Validate the visual experience for each communication to secure optimal customer experience in each channel.



 Operational execution and delivery
   

  




* Own day-to-day production timelines, ensuring campaigns are delivered on time and within agreed guardrails.
* Manage content updates, asset swaps and last-minute adjustments, conducting quality assurance checks before deployment to secure flawless delivery and optimal channel execution.
* Document production steps and maintain clear, structured workflows.



 Performance follow-up and iteration
   

  




* Monitor campaign performance, flag deviations and execute predefined A/B tests and optimization adjustments.
* Support local markets with production-related questions and provide structured feedback to Planned Communications Specialists to inform continuous improvement.


**Qualifications**

**WHO YOU ARE**




 We are looking for people with....
   

  




* A bachelor's degree in business, Marketing, Economics, Communication or related field.
* One to four years’ experience in campaign execution, CRM, owned media or marketing operations–related roles.
* A structured, detail-oriented way of working, with the ability to manage multiple campaigns in parallel.
* Analytical mindset with the ability to monitor performance and translate insights into improvements.



 And people who are...
   

  




* Well organized and comfortable handling multiple, sometimes complex, projects at the same time.
* Creatively minded, with a strong eye for detail, quality, and what makes communications visually effective and coherent across all owned channels.
* Clear, confident communicators who bring a positive, collaborative attitude.
* Curious, solution-oriented, and motivated to continuously improve how things are done.


**Additional Information**

**WHO WE ARE**




 H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.
   

  





 We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future.
   

  





**WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING HERE**




 At H&M Group, we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer our employees attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe.
   

  




* 25% Staff discount on all our H&M Group brands, both in stores and online (H&M, COS, Weekday, Monki, H&M HOME, & Other Stories, and ARKET).
* H&M Incentive Program – HIP



**JOIN US**




 Our uniqueness comes from a combination of many things – our inclusive and collaborative culture, our strong values, and opportunities for growth. But most of all, it’s our people who make us who we are.
   

  





 Take the next step in your career together with us. The journey starts here.
   

  





 This is a  full-time permanent position based in Stockholm, please send your CV in English as soon as possible, by the latest 28th April 2026.
   

  





 \*We are committed to a recruitment process that is fair, equitable, and based on competency. We therefore kindly ask you to not attach a cover letter in your application.

title: Solution Architect
description:
We’re looking for a
 **Solution Architect** 
 to join us at Telia.
   

  

 I’m
 **Marton Sabli** 
 , Head of the
 **Architecture & Roadmap team** 
 , and I’m searching for someone ready to grow with us. We’re a supportive, forward‑thinking team focused on
 **designing secure, scalable and future‑proof OSS, IT Production and Cybersecurity architecture across Telia countries** 
 .
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
 You will own and drive end‑to‑end solution architecture for critical fulfillment, inventory and network build domains, ensuring secure and resilient service delivery.
   

  

* Develop end‑to‑end solution architectures for Fulfillment, Service Order Management, Service & Resource Inventory, and Network Build solutions
* Design architectural flows from customer order to service activation, aligned to target architecture, reference architecture and roadmaps
* Act as a Solution Architect for Network Build orchestration and automation, including work orders, resource build flows, and integration with construction and field service management systems
* Drive the AI introduction in this Technology domain to Increase Autonomous NW operations
* Collaborate within ART (Agile Release Train) with business architects, product management and cross functional teams to ensure architectural compliance and value delivery


**What Will Help You Thrive In This Role**
 You combine domain expertise with stakeholder collaboration and the ability to balance strategic thinking with hands‑on solution design.
   

  

* Working experience in solution architecture within telecom or large-scale IT environments, particularly OSS/BSS and end-to-end service delivery
* Experience and knowledge in at least one of the following domains: Fulfillment, Service & Resource Order Management, Inventory solutions, or Network Build / Network Creation
* Programing experience in at least one major programing language e.g. Java, Python, Java Script and experience in GitHub Copilot
* Solid experience working in agile environments, including ARTs, PI (Product increment) planning, and architectural governance at epic and feature levels
* A relevant university degree in Computer Science or equivalent


**Join us to shape smarter living for people, businesses, and the planet**
 At Telia, we’re a team of
 **15,000 colleagues across the Nordics and Baltics** 
 , passionate about technology and connectivity. As a
 **Leading Telecommunications Operator & Climate Leader** 
 , we drive change through large‑scale, impactful initiatives while building a more sustainable and inclusive society.
   

  

 Here, you can
 **grow your skills, lead meaningful architecture work, and thrive in a collaborative, expert‑driven environment** 
 . Whether you’re deepening your expertise or shaping long‑term roadmaps, Telia is a place to belong and make real impact.
   

  

**What We Offer**
 When you join us, you’ll receive a comprehensive benefits package tailored to your local market, flexible ways of working, and opportunities to influence large‑scale, business‑critical architectures. You’ll also be eligible for a bonus based on your performance and Telia’s results.
   

  

**Ready to make better happen?**
 We look forward to receiving your application by
 **May 11.** 
 We may close the position earlier if we find the right candidate, so don’t wait too long to apply.
   

  

 If you’re selected for the next stage, you’ll be contacted by our
 **Talent Acquisition Partner** 
 . Background and reference checks will be part of the process.
   

  

 Want to know more about this role? Please reach out to
 **Marton Sabli** 
 at
 **marton.sabli@teliacompany.com** 
 .
   

  

**Equal opportunities**
 At Telia, we recognize talent by
 **professional skills and growth potential** 
 . We champion diversity and are committed to building an inclusive workplace where
 **your uniqueness is our strength** 
 .
   

  

**Join us and let’s make better happen – together!**

title: Cloud DevOps Engineer
description:

* AWS

* Airflow

**Meritorious**

 27-04-2026

 Working at Deploja is something entirely different from working at an ordinary consulting company—and we are really proud of that. So, what makes us unique?

title: Account Manager
description:
foodora is part of the Delivery Hero Group, the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience-fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in over 70+ countries worldwide. Headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index.
   

  

**Job Description**
**From groceries and flowers to dog food and phone chargers - these days, you can order just about anything through the foodora app. This level of convenience is made possible by the expertise of our Marketplace team. We're currently on the lookout for our next superstar Account Manager to join this dynamic group. In this role, you'll be the key ingredient in nurturing and strengthening relationships with our existing partners, ensuring that every collaboration becomes a mutual success story! If you want to be a part of reshaping the way people do their shopping, now is your time!**
**Your mission**
 Join our team and create an impact in the quick commerce revolution! We partner with retailers across various industries with a strong focus on grocery partnerships.
   

  

* Manage a dynamic portfolio ranging from influential local businesses to the largest brands within the grocery and retail sector.
* Educate our partners within quick commerce and analyze their performance on our platform to set a joint plan for future success, with a strong focus on developing their digital business.
* Develop a successful collaboration with partners and create a commercial plan with primary focus on order and revenue growth.
* Drive partner engagement and visibility by planning and activating promotions across your portfolio.


 We are looking for a true foodorian who enjoys working in a
 **dynamic** 
 environment and is eager to elevate our team. To thrive in this role, you'll need to bring loads of
 **energy** 
 and
 **drive** 
 - we are not just closing deals, we are creating new consumer behaviors and we need you to take it to the next level!
 **Cooperation** 
 is our secret sauce, and we believe that effective teamwork turns prospects into partners. Your
 **solution-oriented** 
 mindset allows you to see a new approach instead of the bumps in the road. If you’re
 **responsible** 
 and capable of juggling multiple tasks without dropping the ball, you’ll fit right in. Since you’ll be collaborating with various partners, building and maintaining
 **strong relationships** 
 will be your bread and butter, driving our collective success
   

  

**Qualifications**
* Minimum 2 years of working within B2B Sales or Customer Success with good results
* Fluent in Swedish and English
* You have a driver's license
* Any experience from working with or within FMCG is a big plus.


**Additional Information**
* Wellness allowance topped with the opportunity to participate in many work/life balance initiatives at foodora such as wellness hour & reduced price on massage.
* Employee discount at foodora (woho!) and awesome friday breakfasts!
* Occupational pension, incl. premium exemption insurance, accident insurance and life insurance.
* foodora Learning - Monthly learning sessions about different topics, such as the q-commerce industry, negotiation techniques and project management combined with a structured onboarding and inspiring courses within our learning tool Sana
* Great deals at Benify (for e.g. gym membership, e-bikes hotels, audiobooks, streaming services, clothes, kitchen supplies… and so much more!
* Awesome AW’s and (pink) parties!

title: ENGINEERS & MECHANICS - DAY AND NIGHTSHIFT
description:
Join SAS Line Maintenance at Arlanda Airport and help keep our fleet flying safely and efficiently. We maintain SAS aircraft (A320, A330, A350) and support other customers’ fleets.
   

  

 We are now looking for licensed aircraft engineers and experienced aircraft mechanics for both our day- and nightshifts.
   

  

**About The Role**
 As a Licensed Aircraft Engineer or experienced aircraft mechanic, you’ll be part of a dynamic team ensuring aircraft safety and reliability.
   

  

* Performing planned maintenance up to A-checks, including engine and landing gear replacements.
* Troubleshooting and rectifying faulty systems.
* Recording all activities in IT systems.


 What You Bring
   

  

**Must-have**
* Documented experience.
* Driver’s license.
* Strong English communication skills.
* High IT proficiency.


**Nice-to-have**
* EASA Part-66 License B1.1 and/or B2 with type rating.
* Type rating on A320, A330, and/or A350.
* Documented experience in line maintenance.
* Scandinavian language skills.
* Experience with AMOS.


 Why SAS
   

  

**We Make Your Journey Matter**
* Training: From human factors to type ratings, via SAS Technical Training.
* Career growth: Opportunities across SAS.
* Travel perks: Discounted tickets for you, family, and friends.
* Community: SAS Employee Club activities (skiing, golf, motor sports, and more).

 Application Details

* Apply via SAS Career Site with relevant documents.
* Questions (not applications): rekryteringsastech@sas.se

title: Configuration Manager
description:
**With a passion for life**
 Join our diverse teams of passionate people and a career that allows you to develop both personally and professionally. At Getinge we exist to make life-saving technology accessible for more people. To make a true difference for our customers – and to save more lives, we need team players, forward thinkers, and game changers.
   

  

 Are you looking for an inspiring career? You just found it.
   

  

**About This Opportunity**
 At Getinge, we develop life-supporting medical technology used every day by healthcare professionals around the world. We are expanding our Development Tools team and are looking for a Configuration Manager to own and improve how we structure, control, and govern configurations in our requirements and test management setup—working hands-on with tools like IBM Jazz and ClearQuest to drive versioning, change control, traceability, and compliance, while making the everyday tool experience efficient and easy to work in.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
 In this role, your primary responsibility will be to ensure the integrity, consistency, and controlled evolution of system and software configurations across all environments.
   

  

 Examples of what you will do in your daily work:
   

  

* Serve as the owner of configuration management processes, ensuring systems, applications, and infrastructure remain consistent, secure, and compliant
* Manage and control configuration changes, including review, approval, tracking, and rollback procedures
* Automate configuration management tasks using approved tools and scripts to improve reliability and efficiency
* Collaborate with different stakeholders such as teams within the R&D organization, regulatory department, operations, etc.


 This position is based in Solna, Stockholm and offers a hybrid setup with the possibility to work from home 1-2 days a week. Travelling is not expected in this position.
   

  

**You will bring**
 To be successful in this position, we believe that you have the following qualifications.
   

  

* Bachelor/Master of Science in Engineering or equivalent.
* Several years' experience of configuration management within different tools, such as IBM Jazz, Jira
* Experience in working with, basic Linux, REST API, VB-script, Pearl, Java Script
* Fluency in English (Swedish is a plus)


**Valued Qualifications But Not Required**
* Experience from regulated environments (e.g. medical devices)
* Experience with ClearQuest, ClearCase, Gitlab


 In addition to your technical skills, we highly value your personality. We see that you are humble but willing to voice your opinions, and that you value differences in opinion. You are good at collaborating and communicating, with a social side. We also see that you are driven and can work independently in your work. We think you have an ambition for growth and an eagerness for keeping up to date with the latest in configuration management.
   

  

 At Getinge we see ourselves as proactive and self-driven in learning and contributing to the continued development of our products and our organization's capabilities.
   

  

**Why join Getinge**
 Getinge offers a job in a safe and informal work environment, where we appreciate close team collaboration. We keep an open dialogue between leaders and employees, as well as between teams. We are focused on developing people, and together we will create a plan for learning and competence development when you start your career with us.
   

  

 We offer a competitive compensation and benefits package, including wellness allowances, generous family benefits and joint company activities. Everything to ensure we support your well-being and goals.
   

  

**Application**
 Applications will be reviewed continually, and we therefore recommend sending us your application as soon as possible. We kindly ask you to submit your application in our recruitment system by clicking the “apply now” button. Due to GDPR regulations we do not accept applications via e-mail.
   

  

 If you have any questions regarding the role, please contact Hiring Manager Helen Grindberg at helen.grindberg@getinge.com or Recruiter Sofia Ekstrand at sofia.ekstrand@getinge.com .
   

  

 We look forward to receiving your application. We hope you will join us on our journey to become the world’s most desired MedTech company.
   

  

**About Us**
 With a firm belief that every person and community should have access to the best possible care, Getinge provides hospitals and life science institutions with products and solutions aiming to improve clinical results and optimize workflows. The offering includes products and solutions for intensive care, cardiovascular procedures, operating rooms, sterile reprocessing and life science. Getinge employs over 12,000 people worldwide and the products are sold in more than 135 countries.

title: Account Solution Engineer
description:
*To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.*
 Job Category
   

  

 Sales
   

  

 Job Details
   

  

**About Salesforce**
 Salesforce is the #1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all.
   

  

 Ready to level-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce.
   

  

**What is an Account Solution Engineer?**
 Also known as a “Presales”, “Solution Advisor”, “Sales Consultant,” or “Sales Engineer” the Account Solution Engineer is responsible for owning the solutions throughout the sales cycle and delivering thought leadership to companies to transform their customer’s experience. The Solution Engineer is an expert who works alongside their colleagues, participates in building our team culture and supports their aligned sales team to create solutions for prospects and customers.
   

  

 To accomplish this, you should have a strong desire to set the vision translating business objectives into enticing and practical recommendations. The Solution Engineer acts as a trusted advisor to our customers while aiding the customer’s transformational journey.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Strategic Customer Engagement: Partner with sales teams to drive digital transformation initiatives for automotive and manufacturing enterprises, positioning Salesforce as the platform for the agentic enterprise where AI agents work alongside humans to drive business outcomes
