title: Analytics Engineer
description:
**Education changes lives. But tech hasn’t lived up to its promise, yet. At Kognity, we’re here to change that.** 




 We’re a 125-person EdTech scale-up powering learning in 120+ countries. Our intelligent platform combines rich pedagogy with smart AI to help students and teachers thrive, from international schools to US high schools.
 



  





**Why Kognity is the place to be:** 




 ✨
 **Educational Innovation** 
 – Lead the next wave of AI-powered learning solutions
 



 🌍
 **Global Reach** 
 – Our platform is used in 120+ countries across international and US markets
 



 🤝
 **Collaborative Culture** 
 – Join a smart, ambitious team that values impact over ego
 



**💪 High-Performing Teams** 
 – Work with sharp, driven colleagues across product, engineering, and AI who raise the bar every day.
 



  





**The Role** 




 You will own the end-to-end analytics engineering layer - turning raw data into well-modeled, trusted metrics and scalable, business-ready data products that power decision-making across the company.
 



 You’ll join a small team with a strong and stable data foundation, modern tooling, and a clear strategy in place. As we move into the next phase, you’ll play a key role in building a self-service data platform and advancing a metrics-first approach. With high ownership, a flat organisation, and cutting-edge technology, this is a rare opportunity to shape the future of analytics in a company that is ambitious and ready to scale.
 



  





**What you’ll do:** 



* Design, implement, and maintain scalable data models and transformations to deliver clean, reliable business-ready datasets.
* Collaborate with other analytics engineers to integrate new data sources, automate workflows, and ensure reliable, high-performing data pipelines.
* Develop and maintain a semantic layer to standardize key metrics and definitions across the organization.
* Implement data quality checks, testing frameworks, and governance practices to ensure accuracy and consistency.
* Partner with stakeholders across product, finance and sales teams to translate business needs into robust analytical and modeling solutions.
* Continuously improve tools, processes, and architecture by applying modern analytics engineering best practices and actively experimenting with AI.



  





**What we’re looking for** 




**Experience that drives impact:** 



* Experience with ELT/ETL workflows, version control (Git), and data governance.
* Strong SQL and dbt experience, with proven skills in building and maintaining dimensional data models.
* Experience in analytics or data engineering with modern cloud data stacks (e.g. Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift).



**Ways of working:** 



* You combine strong business acumen with technical depth, understanding how metrics drive decisions and company outcomes.
* You are collaborative and proactive in engaging both technical and non-technical stakeholders, building strong cross-functional relationships rather than working in isolation.
* You take ownership and are self-driven - you move things forward without waiting for detailed instructions and thrive in ambiguity.
* You are detail-oriented with a passion for problem-solving, continuous improvement, and raising the bar for quality and standards.



  





**Our Values** 




 🏅
 **We take ownership** 
 – We take initiative and act with self-leadership. We don’t wait for someone else to solve problems we see.
 



 🤖
 **We leverage AI** 
 – We apply AI to enhance creativity, decisions, and execution to allow for impact maximization.
 



 💡
 **We drive customer value** 
 – Success for our customers drives our progress. We create value for them in everything we do.
 



 🗣️
 **We are transparent** 
 – We are radically transparent with opinions and feedback, and we share information widely.
 



 💙
 **We take care of ourselves and each other** 
 – We work hard and passionately, but also prioritise our own well-being, and that of our colleagues.
 



  





**Benefits** 




 🏡 Truly Hybrid — work from our Stockholm office when you like.
 



 💰 ITP Pension Plan with Nordnet.
 



 💆 Yearly budget of 5,000 SEK to spend on health-related services.
 



 🌴 30 days of paid vacation every year.
 



 🤒 Full pay sick leave starting on day 1.
 



  





**Our Interview Process** 




 Our hiring process is all about you. Show us your skills, tackle real-world challenges, and get a real feel for life at Kognity. Expect case studies, honest conversations, and plenty of chances to shine.
 


1. Discovery Call with a Recruiter
2. Hiring Manager Hangout
3. Case study
4. Values Interview
5. Leadership discussion



 Every qualified person will be evaluated regardless of age, gender, identity, nationality, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disability status or religion. We’re committed to building a diverse, inclusive team and welcome people of all backgrounds, experiences, perspectives, and abilities.

title: Partner Marketing Manager - AWS
description:
At Snowflake, we are powering the era of the agentic enterprise. To usher in this new era, we seek AI-native thinkers across every function who are energized by the opportunity to reinvent how they work. You don’t just use tools; you possess an innate curiosity, treating AI as a high-trust collaborator that is core to how you solve problems and accelerate your impact. We look for low-ego individuals who thrive in dynamic and fast-moving environments and move with an experimental mindset — who rapidly test emerging capabilities to discover simpler, more powerful ways to deliver results. At Snowflake, your role isn't just to execute a function, but to help redefine the future of how work gets done.
   

  

 Snowflake is seeking a dynamic and results-oriented
 **Partner Marketing Program Manager -** 
 to join our EMEA partner marketing team. In this pivotal role, you will be instrumental in maximizing the value and impact of our alliances with one of our cloud partners (AWS).
   

  

 This role focuses heavily on execution, governance, and enablement, ensuring our high-touch field activities with AWS are flawlessly managed and aligned with our broader strategic Go-To-Market (GTM) motions. You will serve as the dedicated point of contact for our Alliances and Field Teams to ensure local field events are funded, approved, and executed without bottlenecks. As part of the role, you will also work on marketing - through campaigns aligned to Snowflake’s top priorities.
   

  

 Success in this role hinges on exceptional cross-functional collaboration. You will work closely with marketing, alliances, and sales teams.
   

  

 This position has a
 **hybrid work schedule** 
 requiring
 **3 days per week in Snowflake’s London office.**
**AS A PARTNER MARKETING PROGRAM MANAGER - AWS AT SNOWFLAKE, YOU WILL:**
* Manage effective MDF spend against AWS and Snowflake’s priorities
* Measure Success: Establish goals and track the performance of all partner marketing initiatives to make a meaningful difference in Snowflake's business.
* Build Pipeline: Design, execute, and manage innovative marketing through campaigns for our partners that deliver measurable results.
* Influence and Align Cross-Functionally: Embed our cloud partnerships within Snowflake’s highly cross-functional working teams, collaborating across cloud alliances, product marketing, field marketing, demand gen, content, PR, and social.


**OUR IDEAL SENIOR PARTNER MARKETING MANAGER WILL HAVE:**
* Relevant Experience: 5+ years of marketing or alliances experience, preferably high-growth, successful, B2B tech companies. Product or partner marketing background preferred.
* Strategic Vision & Tactical Execution: Exhibits the ability to think strategically about full-funnel pipeline development while also being highly adept at hands-on execution and tactical problem-solving to ensure initiatives deliver tangible results.
* Exceptional Influencer & Communicator: A natural storyteller and persuasive influencer, skilled at crafting clear, concise, and impactful messages for diverse internal and external audiences.
* An 'Own It' Mentality: A proactive mindset with a bias for action and the ability to act decisively, take initiative, and move projects forward efficiently.
* Cross-Functional Excellence: Proven ability to thrive in a highly cross-functional and matrixed environment with numerous internal and external stakeholders.
* Big Picture Thinking: The ability to think big and re-imagine how Snowflake can deliver value to customers in collaboration with our partner ecosystem.
* Education: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, or a related field.


 Snowflake is growing fast, and we’re scaling our team to help enable and accelerate our growth. We are looking for people who share our values, challenge ordinary thinking, and push the pace of innovation while building a future for themselves and Snowflake.
   

  

 How do you want to make your impact?
   

  

 For jobs located in the United States, please visit the job posting on the Snowflake Careers Site for salary and benefits information: careers.snowflake.com

title: Senior Software Engineer
description:
⚡️
 **What We Do**
 Flower is Flexible Power. We are a next-gen energy company leveraging AI and machine learning to make renewable energy stable and always available – even when the sun isn’t shining and the wind isn’t blowing.
   

  

 Through smart optimization and trading of energy assets like wind and solar farms, battery systems, and EV chargers, we make renewable energy reliable and predictable, leading the charge towards the energy system of tomorrow.
   

  

 🌟
 **Who We Are**
 Tech company at heart – purpose in our DNA. Flower consists of a diverse group of innovative individuals with a strong desire to improve the state of the world.
   

  

 At Flower, we believe trust, collaboration and diversity are essential to not only create an inclusive work environment, but also drive career growth. By embracing varying perspectives, we allow creativity and progress to flourish.
   

  

 To accelerate towards our goal of becoming the pioneering force powering the energy system of tomorrow, we are now looking for a passionate and skilled Senior Software Engineer to join our Asset Optimisation domain.
   

  

 👩‍💻
 **About The Role**
 As a Senior Software Engineer at Flower, you will play a key role in designing, building, and evolving our settlement system - the backbone that ensures accurate, transparent, and traceable transactions between trading activities and asset owners.
   

  

 You will work at the intersection of engineering, finance, and trading, developing a robust transaction-based system that supports invoicing, financial follow-up, and trading insights. This role combines hands-on development with high-level system design, giving you the opportunity to shape both the technical architecture and the way we work as a team.
   

  

 You’ll be part of a fast-moving and evolving environment where your ability to bring structure, clarity, and technical excellence will have a direct impact on the business.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
* Be one of the main drivers in the design and development of Flower’s settlement system
* Design system architecture, data flows, and transaction structures to ensure precision, traceability, and transparency
* Build and maintain scalable, robust, and maintainable solutions
* Drive the evolution of the system from its current state to a more structured and rigorous platform
* Collaborate closely with stakeholders across trading, finance, and commercial teams
* Ensure systems support invoicing, revenue distribution, and financial follow-up
* Improve processes related to settlement, data clarity, and reporting
* Contribute to and establish best practices, technical standards, and ways of working
* Act as a senior technical contributor, balancing hands-on coding with architectural decisions
* Mentor team members and provide technical guidance
* Help the team navigate ambiguity while driving structure and high-quality delivery


**Who You Are**
* 8+ years of experience in software development
* Strong experience designing and building systems from scratch, including architecture and implementation
* Solid understanding of system design, data flows, and scalable architectures
* Experience working in cloud-based environments
* Experience with transaction-heavy systems (e.g. fintech, trading, banking, betting, or similar) is highly valued
* Comfortable switching between hands-on development and high-level design
* Take ownership and drive technical initiatives end-to-end
* Thrive in fast-paced, evolving environments and can bring structure where needed
* Communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders
* Enjoy mentoring others and acting as a senior figure within a team


 📍 Location
   

  

 Our beautiful office is located in the heart of Södermalm, just a short walk from Slussen subway station. We encourage in-office collaboration but support a hybrid work model.
   

  

 ✉️ Apply
   

  

 Our corporate language is English, as we have about 30 nationalities in the office. We therefore appreciate it if you could submit your CV in English.
   

  

 Throughout the recruitment process you will meet with the Talent Partner, the Head of Evaluation and Control, the Team and VP of Engineering.
   

  

 We look forward to hearing from you!
   

  

 —-
   

  

*We kindly but firmly decline any engagement in recruitment assistance for our hiring processes. This includes partnership offers or the sale of recruitment tools.*

title: Senior Data Engineer | AI & Data | Saab
description:
*Vill du bygga nästa generations dataplattform med senaste teknikerna, där målet är att skala både affärsvärde och robusthet i system som bidrar till samhällets säkerhet? Vill du ha en nyckelroll där data och AI inte bara är buzzwords, utan långsiktiga satsningsområden med verklig tyngd? Då kan detta vara rätt nästa steg för dig - skicka in din ansökan redan idag!* 




  





**Din roll** 




 På Saab pågår just nu en stor satsning på AI och Data. AI är en viktig del, men utan en välbyggd, skalbar och tillförlitlig dataförmåga stannar utvecklingen. Som
 **Senior Data Engineer** 
 i AI & Data bygger du grunden som gör datadrivna beslut, effektiva interna arbetsflöden och nästa generations produkter möjliga.
 



  





 Rollen är operativ och hands-on, men med ett tydligt inslag av tekniskt ledarskap. Du designar, bygger och vidareutvecklar datalösningar som används i produktion, och du är den som säkerställer att arkitekturbeslut, mönster och tekniska val håller över tid. Du arbetar nära dataflöden, pipelines, databaser och molnbaserade miljöer, och bidrar till att höja nivån på hela dataekosystemet.
 



  





 Du samarbetar tätt med andra Data Engineers, AI-ingenjörer och plattformsteam, och tar en aktiv roll i att utveckla tekniska arbetssätt, gemensamma standarder och arkitekturer. Du förväntas också vara en kunskapskälla och mentor för kollegor som är tidigare i sin karriär - någon som lyfter teamet genom att dela erfarenhet, driva tekniska diskussioner och bidra till att bygga en stark teknisk kultur.
 



  





**Din profil** 




 Vi söker dig som har flera års erfarenhet av data engineering i produktionsmiljöer och som är van att ta ägande över tekniska lösningar från design till drift. Du är trygg i din tekniska grund, har ett starkt systemtänk och är bekväm med att fatta och motivera tekniska beslut som påverkar hela plattformen.
 



  





 Du har god erfarenhet av molnplattformar som AWS och Azure, och har arbetat med moderna datamiljöer där skalbarhet, datakvalitet och automation är centralt. Du har sannolikt arbetat med orkestrering av dataflöden, har förståelse för principer som Data Mesh, Data Products och semantisk modellering, och ser hur dessa mönster kan tillämpas i praktiken för att skapa verkligt värde.
 



  





 Rollen passar dig som vill vara med och sätta riktningen för hur data hanteras och används i en av Sveriges mest tekniskt ambitiösa organisationer, och som trivs bäst när du får kombinera operativt arbete med strategisk påverkan. Rollen har sin primära utgångspunkt i Stockholm eller Göteborg, där huvuddelen av teamen och den tekniska miljön finns.
 



  





**Kvalifikationer:** 




  




* Kandidatexamen inom datateknik, IT, systemutveckling eller motsvarande
* Flera års erfarenhet av data engineering i produktionsmiljöer
* Stark erfarenhet av programmering i Python, SQL och/eller Scala
* Erfarenhet av molnplattformar (AWS, Azure) och modern datainfrastruktur
* Erfarenhet av att designa och bygga datapipelines, ETL/ELT-flöden och datamodeller
* Erfarenhet av Linux-baserade utvecklingsmiljöer
* God kommunikativ förmåga på svenska och engelska



  





**Meriterande:** 




  




* Erfarenhet av orkestrering och workflow-verktyg som Airflow, Dagster eller liknande
* Förståelse för Data Mesh, Data Products, semantisk modellering och datakatalogisering
* Erfarenhet av IaC-verktyg (Terraform, Pulumi) och CI/CD för datalösningar



  





 Denna position kräver att du blir godkänd i en säkerhetsprövning i enlighet med den svenska säkerhetsskyddslagen.
 



  





**Vad du blir en del av** 




**Common Tech** 
 har i uppdrag att skapa gemensamma tekniska förutsättningar för hela Saab. Genom att utveckla och förvalta gemensamma plattformar, tekniska ramverk och arbetssätt möjliggör Common Tech ett effektivt, enhetligt och långsiktigt teknikutvecklingsarbete över hela organisationen. Målet är att ge Saabs team rätt tekniska grund för att kunna utveckla avancerade produkter med hög kvalitet, säkerhet och tillförlitlighet.
 



  





**AI & Data** 
 är en av flera avdelningar inom Common Tech och ansvarar för att bygga och vidareutveckla Saabs gemensamma förmåga inom data, analys och artificiell intelligens. Här skapas plattformar, verktyg och tekniska lösningar som möjliggör storskalig datahantering, avancerad analys och användning av AI i verksamhetskritiska miljöer. Fokus ligger på att bygga robusta, skalbara och återanvändbara AI- och datakapabiliteter som kan användas brett över flera produkter, system och affärsområden.
 



  





 Inom AI & Data kombineras djup teknisk kompetens med ett långsiktigt och strategiskt perspektiv. Arbetet handlar inte om enskilda applikationer, utan om att skapa gemensamma tekniska förutsättningar - från dataplattformar och AI-infrastruktur till modeller, pipelines och arbetssätt - som andra team kan lita på och bygga vidare på.
 



  





**Saab** 
 är ett ledande försvars- och säkerhetsföretag med ett tydligt uppdrag: att bidra till att skydda människor och samhällen. Med omkring 20 000 medarbetare världen över utvecklar Saab avancerad teknik i nära samarbete mellan människor, kompetenser och discipliner. Företaget präglas av en inkluderande kultur där samarbete, lärande och långsiktig utveckling står i centrum.
 



  





*Dessa positioner kräver godkänd säkerhetsprövning enligt den svenska säkerhetsskyddslagen.* 




  





**Ansökan** 




 Den här rekryteringsprocessen hanteras av Jefferson Wells för SAABS räkning, vilket innebär att du kommer att bli anställd direkt av SAAB.
 



  





 Vi tar ej emot ansökningar via email pga GDPR.
 



  





**Urval och intervjuer sker löpande. Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!**

title: Senior Sales Engineer (Sweden - Stockholm)
description:
**Recruitment Fraud Alert**
 We’ve learned that scammers are impersonating Commvault team members—including HR and leadership—via email or text. These bad actors may conduct fake interviews and ask for personal information, such as your social security number.
   

  

**What To Know**
* Commvault does not conduct interviews by email or text.
* We will never ask you to submit sensitive documents (including banking information, SSN, etc) before your first day.


 If you suspect a recruiting scam, please contact us at wwrecruitingteam@commvault.com
   

  

**About Commvault**
 Commvault (NASDAQ: CVLT) is the gold standard in cyber resilience. The company empowers customers to uncover, take action, and rapidly recover from cyberattacks – keeping data safe and businesses resilient. The company’s unique AI-powered platform combines best-in-class data protection, exceptional data security, advanced data intelligence, and lightning-fast recovery across any workload or cloud at the lowest TCO. For over 25 years, more than 100,000 organizations and a vast partner ecosystem have relied on Commvault to reduce risks, improve governance, and do more with data.
   

  

 Commvault Senior Sales Engineer (SSE) is a pre-sales support role primarily focused on providing technical sales support to Commvault’s partners and channel. The role supports partner-focused sales teams deploying their skills to influence and drive though the sales cycle. The position requires a strong technical contributor who understands how their decisions impact and influence customer value while at time driving revenue for Commvault.
   

  

 The SSE is seen as a trusted advisor at all levels within an account (C-Suite, technical stakeholders, etc.). as well as, be a team leader, mentor, teach, and contribute to overall success of Commvault. The SSE drives or supports complex sales opportunities by translating business requirements into solution technical requirements; coordinating technical expertise; sizing, scoping, defining delivery and deployment approach, timeline, and required resources; coordinating demonstrations and proof-of-concept; and supporting internal deal acceptance.
   

  

**What You'll Do**
* Collaborate with the regional Pre-Sales team reporting to Regional Pre-Sales Leader
* Present and demonstrate the Commvault software suite of products through the use of appropriate media – presentation, white board and demonstrations, etc.
* Work with internal departments (sales, support, marketing, channel), as appropriate, to ensure success during sales campaigns and/or to ensure the partner experience is positive
* Assist in achieving annual revenue goals in line with the Commvault’s regional business plan
* Attend meetings, training and maintain regular communication with the regional / global Pre-Sales community and other technical and development functions
* Be an inspiration leader within the Pre-Sales division and a go-to resource
* Actively participate in all Sales and regional meetings, QBRs, adding value and technical leadership on all topics (field marketing, qualifying, etc.).


**Who You Are**
* Demonstrably experienced within Software / SaaS industry in a similar capacity. Ideally within Data Management / Security and Cyber Resiliency
* Wide range of technical knowledge of data management inclusive of applications, cloud, compliance, disaster recovery, tape infrastructure, storage arrays, storage networking, networking protocols, and general connectivity in multi-platform enterprise environments
* A dynamic, fast paced individual who is motivated by success and takes the responsibility of owning issues to resolution. Understands the true meaning of “ownership” and “will do”
* Exceptional internal and client centric interpersonal skills in a commercial situation
* Shown success in taking care of Enterprise clients and working on high level deals
* Experience of working on sophisticated RFPs
* Competence with prioritising work and balancing workloads
* Fluent English and Swedish verbal and written language skills
* Willingness to travel


**You’ll Love Working Here Because**
* High income earning opportunities based on self performance
* Opportunity for Presidents Club
* RSU and Employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)
* Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing
* Sales training in MEDDIC and Command of the Message
* Market-leading cyber resilience platform


 Commvault is an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are always committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status and we will not discriminate against on the basis of such characteristics or any other status protected by the laws or regulations in the locations where we work.
   

  

 Commvault’s goal is to make interviewing inclusive and accessible to all candidates and employees. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation to participate in the interview process or apply for a position at Commvault, please email accommodations@commvault.com For any inquiries not related to an accommodation please reach out to wwrecruitingteam@commvault.com.
   

  

 Commvault's Privacy Policy

title: Lead, Account Solution Engineer - Retail & Consumer Goods
description:
*To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.*
 Job Category
   

  

 Sales
   

  

 Job Details
   

  

**About Salesforce**
 Salesforce is the #1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all.
   

  

 Ready to level-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce.
   

  

 Salesforce is the #1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn't a buzzword — it's a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all.
   

  

 Ready to level-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You're in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce.
   

  

**What is an Account Solution Engineer?**
 Also known as a "Presales", "Solution Advisor", "Sales Consultant," or "Sales Engineer" the Account Solution Engineer is responsible for owning the solutions throughout the sales cycle and delivering thought leadership to companies to transform their customer's experience. The Solution Engineer is an expert who works alongside their colleagues, participates in building our team culture and supports their aligned sales team to create solutions for prospects and customers.
   

  

 To accomplish this, you should have a strong desire to set the vision translating business objectives into enticing and practical recommendations. The Solution Engineer acts as a trusted advisor to our customers while aiding the customer's transformational journey.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Strategic Customer Engagement: Partner with sales teams to drive digital transformation initiatives for retail and consumer goods enterprises, positioning Salesforce as the platform for the agentic enterprise where AI agents work alongside humans to drive business outcomes
* Solution Architecture & Design: Lead technical discovery sessions to understand complex business processes across retail operations, omnichannel commerce, supplier networks, and customer experience, translating requirements into comprehensive Salesforce solutions spanning across the portfolio
* Agentic AI Evangelism: Demonstrate and articulate how Agentforce and autonomous AI agents can transform customer-facing and internal operations—from intelligent service agents handling customer inquiries to autonomous SDR agents qualifying leads and autonomous sales coaches optimizing rep performance
* Value-Based Selling: Build and present compelling business cases that connect technical solutions to measurable business outcomes, focusing on revenue growth through platform expansion, cross-cloud opportunities, and demonstrating clear ROI
* Demo/Workshop/Proof of Concept Execution: Design and deliver impactful demonstrations, customer workshops and pilots that showcase the art of the possible, driving operational efficiency and customer engagement in retail and consumer goods contexts
* Trusted Advisor Relationship Building: Establish yourself as a strategic technology advisor to C-level and senior technical stakeholders, understanding their industry challenges around unified commerce, loyalty management, demand forecasting, supply chain visibility, and evolving consumer expectations
* Platform Expansion & Land-and-Expand Strategy: Identify opportunities to expand Salesforce footprint within existing accounts by uncovering new use cases across departments and business units, driving new revenue through strategic platform growth
* Collaboration & Knowledge Sharing: Work closely with product specialists, industry advisors, architects, customer success teams and partners to ensure seamless handoffs and contribute to the broader solution engineering community through knowledge sharing and best practice documentation


**Key Attributes & Experience**
* Solution Engineering Excellence: Proven track record as a pre-sales solution engineer, sales engineer, or technical consultant successfully supporting complex software sales cycles
* Business Acumen: Demonstrated ability to think strategically about business value rather than just technical features, with experience building business cases, ROI models, and value realization frameworks that resonate with executive buyers
* Growth Mindset: Intellectual curiosity and commitment to continuous learning in a rapidly evolving technology landscape, particularly around AI/ML, data platforms, and industry-specific innovations
* Customer-Centric: Consultative approach with exceptional listening skills and the ability to ask insightful discovery questions that uncover both stated and unstated customer needs
* AI & Automation Fluency: Understanding of how artificial intelligence, machine learning, and autonomous agents are reshaping business processes, with ability to translate technical AI capabilities into business outcomes
* Communication skills: Polished communication and presentation skills with the confidence to engage C-suite executives, technical architects, and operational managers alike, adapting your message and medium to each audience
* Collaborative Team Player: Experience working in matrix organizations, partnering effectively with account executives, customer success managers, product teams, technical architects as well as external partners to drive customer outcomes
* Language Skills: Swedish and Professional English proficiency required. Salesforce company language is English incl. corporate communication and meetings.
* Proven Track Record: History of meeting or exceeding sales quotas through technical contribution, with metrics demonstrating impact on win rates, deal size, and sales cycle velocity


**Preferred Skills And Experience**
* Industry Expertise: Experience in retail or consumer goods sectors, with understanding of industry-specific challenges e.g. omnichannel commerce, loyalty and personalization programs, demand planning, supplier collaboration, and the shift toward direct-to-consumer and unified commerce models
* Prior experience in Business, Computer Science, MIS, or equivalent fields
* Hands-on experience with Salesforce platform configuration, including exposure to Flow, Lightning Web Components, or Apex development
* Previous experience and/or certification in Salesforce solutions such as Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, Commerce Cloud, Marketing Cloud, Loyalty Management, or B2B/B2C Commerce
* Understanding of modern data architecture concepts including data lakes, real-time integration, API management, and master data management
* Experience with AI/ML platforms, prompt engineering, or implementing intelligent automation solutions
* Knowledge of retail industry standards and technologies (e.g., POS systems, ERP integrations, product information management, and e-commerce platforms)
* Exposure to MuleSoft, Tableau, or other Salesforce ecosystem products
* Experience with value engineering methodologies, business case development tools, or value realization frameworks
* Previous experience selling or implementing large-scale ERP, CRM, or digital transformation platforms (SAP, Oracle, Microsoft Dynamics)
* Understanding of European data privacy regulations (GDPR) and retail industry compliance requirements
* Project management or Agile methodology certifications
* Experience in presenting and hosting webinars or public speaking
* Active participation in Salesforce community, Trailhead accomplishments, or speaking engagements at industry events
* Fluent in Swedish and English + Right to work in Sweden


 Unleash Your Potential
   

  

 When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and
 *be your best* 
 , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can
 *do your best* 
 . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world.
   

  

 Accommodations
   

  

 If you require assistance due to a disability applying for open positions please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form.
   

  

 Posting Statement
   

  

 Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: SAP Masterdata Solution Engineer
description:
Just a kitchen, some might think. But at Nobia, we recognise its deeper essence. It's more than a space – it's the very soul of a home where mornings are greeted and evenings wind down. It is the place where we connect and unite, share love and memories, recharge and let new ideas come to life. Kitchens set the stage for the stories of tomorrow. Join us in our mission of Designing Kitchens for Life!
   

  

**Build the foundation for the future of kitchen manufacturing at Nobia**
 Do you want to create the data foundation that powers highly automated and scalable production? Nobia is now looking for a SAP Master Data Solution Engineer who wants to take end-to-end responsibility for master data in a modern SAP environment.
   

  

**About The Role**
 You ensure that product data, structures, and configuration logic are accurate, reliable, and fit for both business and production. The role is central in the collaboration between IT, business, and manufacturing – from idea to finished kitchen solution.
   

  

**Your mission**
* Design, manage, and continuously improve master data solutions in SAP
* Design, implement, and maintain robust master data structures based on and in collaboration with other IT and Business teams
* Ensure data consistency, quality, and compliance with enterprise standards and SAP best practices.
* Drive continuous improvement of master data processes and support the development of scalable data models aligned with business and system evolution.
* Develop, maintain, and optimize SAP Variant Configuration models to support complex product configuration requirements.
* Partner with stakeholders across R&D, Product Implementation, local Master Data teams, and Operations to understand requirements and translate them into robust SAP solutions.
* Provide expert guidance on best practices related to master data, variant configuration, and data lifecycle management.
* Take part in different test cycles to support with master data solution expertise when necessary
* Support Data Quality and Governance with SAP master data expertise
* Provide support and expert consultation to business users and project teams.
* Specify, validate, and troubleshoot integrations impacting master data and variant configuration, ensuring correct data flows between SAP and connected systems.
* Document solutions and support the organization with expertise and guidance


**We Are Looking For Someone Who**
* Has at least 5 years of experience working with SAP master data and product structures
* Has a solid understanding of material data, product structures, business partner (customer/vendor) and data quality
* Is analytical, detail-oriented, and enjoys solving complex problems
* Communicates clearly and collaborates effectively across teams
* Fluency in English.


**Nice to have**
* Experience with product configuration in SAP
* Understanding of production, sales, or logistics processes
* Experience working close to both technology and business stakeholders


**Why Nobia?**
* You build the foundation for future industrial kitchen solutions
* You make real impact in a business where data is mission critical
* You join transformation, not just maintenance


**Our story**
 Our story is something we are proud of. We foster an open, supportive and innovative work environment where we encourage each other to reach our full potential – for both personal and professional growth – across the group, from the Nordics to the UK.
   

  

 Read more about us at: https://www.nobia.com/about-us/
   

  

**Do you think we are a great match?**
 We look forward to receiving your application before May 10, 2026.
   

  

 If you have any questions about the position or the recruitment process, don’t hesitate to contact us.

title: Account Executive till Projektportalen – B2B-SaaS i tillväxt
description:
****Vill du vara med på Projektportalens tillväxtresa?****
 Vi är på en spännande tillväxtresa och rör oss snabbt framåt. Vi bygger ett team av ambitiösa individer, arbetar nära varandra, har ett starkt kundfokus och en entreprenöriell anda.
   

  

 Vi samarbetar med bygg- och installationsföretag – från snabbväxande entreprenörer till etablerade aktörer – och hjälper dem att modernisera hur projekt drivs och följs upp.
   

  

**Är du vår nästa kollega?**
****Om Projektportalen****
 Projektportalen är en svensk SaaS-plattform för projekthantering, utvecklad specifikt för bygg- och installationsbranschen. Vi hjälper företag att planera, genomföra och följa upp projekt över hela organisationen – och omvandlar komplexa projektflöden till ett strukturerat och förutsägbart arbetssätt.
   

  

 Med mobil- och webbapplikationer för tidrapportering, ÄTA, dokumentation, realtidsrapportering och djupa integrationer med ekonomisystem, kopplar Projektportalen samman arbetsplatsen med kontoret. Resultatet är mindre friktion i det dagliga arbetet och bättre kontroll och beslutsfattande genom hela projektets livscykel.
   

  

 Vi är ett snabbt växande B2B SaaS-bolag som ligger i framkant inom AI - både i vår vardag samt i vår produkt. Här får du möjlighet att arbeta med modern teknik och lösningar som skapar verkligt affärsvärde.
   

  

 Sedan sommaren 2025 backas vi av
 **Monterro** 
 , Nordens ledande investerare inom B2B-SaaS.
   

  

****Rollen – Account Executive****
 Vi söker Account Executives på olika nivåer och välkomnar ansökningar från juniora kandidater med cirka 3 års erfarenhet, samt mer erfarna och seniora kandidater.
   

  

 Som Account Executive på Projektportalen ansvarar du för att vinna nya kunder och stärka vår position inom bygg- och installationsbranschen. Du äger hela säljprocessen och fungerar som en betrodd rådgivare till beslutsfattare, med fokus på att säkerställa långsiktigt värde för varje kund. Det här är en roll för dig som vill:
   

  

* ta stort eget ansvar
* arbeta i ett snabbrörligt bolag där saker byggs medan vi växer
* ha tydlig påverkan på både intäkter, erbjudande och arbetssätt


****Dina arbetsuppgifter****
* Identifiera, kontakta och stänga affärer gentemot nya kunder inom bygg- och installationsbranschen
* Äga hela säljcykeln – från första kontakt och demo till förhandling och avslut
* Genomföra kvalificerade produktdemonstrationer och tydligt koppla funktionalitet till affärsnytta
* Förstå kundernas utmaningar och visa hur Projektportalen förbättrar projektexekvering och samarbete
* Omsätta kund- och affärsinsikter till skarpare positionering, budskap och säljmetodik
* Bidra med strukturerad marknadsfeedback som påverkar både tillväxtprioriteringar och produktutveckling


****Vem är du?****
 Vi värdesätter driv, mindset och resultat. Du passar bra för rollen om du har:
   

  

* Erfarenhet av uppsökande B2B-försäljning med hög aktivitet
* Ett entreprenöriellt förhållningssätt – du trivs i miljöer där allt ännu inte är på plats, är en doer och tar egna initiativ
* Höga prestationskrav och drivs av tillväxt, påverkan och mätbara resultat
* Förmåga att snabbt sätta dig in i både tekniska produkter och kundernas affär
* Lätt för att bygga förtroende och kommunicera professionellt med seniora beslutsfattare
* Ett starkt genomförandefokus – du prioriterar, arbetar strukturerat och bygger vidare på det som fungerar
* En tydlig teamplayer som värdesätter samarbete, delar med dig av insikter och bidrar till lagets gemensamma framgång
* Akademisk utbildning eller motsvarande eftergymnasial utbildning
* Arbetat strukturerat i CRM- och/eller säljstödsystem


**Meriterande**
* Erfarenhet av eller stort intresse för bygg-, installations- eller fastighetsbranschen är meriterande
* Erfarenhet av uppsökande försäljning av B2B-SaaS är meriterande


****Varför Projektportalen?****
 Projektportalen bygger en långsiktig och strategisk plattform för byggbranschen. Du kliver in i ett skede där din prestation gör tydlig skillnad, arbetar nära VD och grundare, och blir en del av en fokuserad tillväxtresa där kommersiella resultat direkt påverkar bolagets utveckling.
   

  

 Din initiala roll ska ses som den första delen av din resa tillsammans med oss. Du kommer få mer ansvar löpande och ha möjlighet att avancera allteftersom att vi växer.
   

  

****Placering****
 Tjänsten är baserad på vårt kontor i
 **Stockholm** 
 . För att lyckas i rollen krävs nära samarbete, snabb onboarding och stark kulturell matchning – därför arbetar vi tätt tillsammans på plats.
   

  

**Tror du att vi är en match?**
 Vi intervjuar löpande – vi ser fram emot din ansökan.

title: Senior Backend Engineer - Subscriptions
description:
The Subscriptions Mission team builds and evolves the systems that help listeners discover, try, and subscribe to Spotify. From awareness to activation, retention, and conversion, we create seamless experiences that connect users with the value of Spotify at every step of their journey.
   

  

 You’ll join a team within the Subscriptions R&D organization, focused on shaping the full user journey at Spotify. We work on how people discover Spotify, understand its value, and ultimately become long-term listeners. By bringing together engineering, product, design, research, and marketing, we move quickly to build meaningful, scalable experiences that reach millions of users worldwide.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

* Design and architect backend systems that power new user-facing features across the subscriptions journey
* Build, deploy, and maintain scalable services with a focus on high availability and low latency
* Collaborate closely with mobile engineers and cross-functional partners across Product, Data Science, User Research, and Design
* Take ownership of services in production, including monitoring, reliability, and participating in an on-call rotation
* Contribute to technical direction and improve system architecture to support long-term scalability
* Support and mentor engineers on the team, helping raise the bar for engineering quality and collaboration


 Who You Are
   

  

* You have experience building backend systems using Java and are comfortable working across modern backend technologies
* You have strong computer science fundamentals and experience developing complex, distributed systems at scale
* You have worked with cloud platforms such as GCP or AWS and understand how to design cloud-native architectures
* You are experienced in designing and developing APIs and systems in collaboration with stakeholders
* You are comfortable working across cross-functional teams and can independently drive projects forward
* You have experience running services in production and understand operational ownership, reliability, and performance
* You are curious, adaptable, and motivated by solving meaningful problems that impact millions of users


 Where You'll Be
   

  

* This role is based in London or Stockholm
* We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.


 Spotify is an equal opportunity employer. You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be forward-thinking! So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.
   

  

 At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. We have ways to request reasonable accommodations during the interview process and help assist in what you need. If you need accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know - we’re here to support you in any way we can.
   

  

 Spotify transformed music listening forever when we launched in 2008. Our mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the chance to enjoy and be passionate about these creators. Everything we do is driven by our love for music and podcasting. Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service.

title: Client Executive - Liability (Large & Complex)
description:
**Do you have passion for building enduring client relationships and delivering tailored solutions?** 




 WTW is growing and following our success we are now expanding the Liability team within the Large & Complex team with a Client Executive having experience within liability insurance solutions.
 



  





 This is a great opportunity to join a growth journey in an international company, working with the most exciting clients in Sweden.
 



  





**The Role** 




 As our new Client Executive - Liability, you have responsibility for managing the client relationship and delivering profitability. You leverage WTW resources to provide coordinated service and placement to meet the client’s insurance and risk management needs. The successful candidate will have responsibility for a portfolio of accounts handling all aspects of clients’ insurance programs and risk management in a timely, technically, accurate and compliant manner. This will include but not be restricted to new business enquiries; client renewals; and broking.
 



  





**About You** 



* You have extensive experience in liability insurance within broking, underwriting and/or claims, with knowledge of the markets, risks and products. Background as a risk engineer or risk consultant is also advantageous.
* You understand the liability industry and can translate this understanding into tailored client insurance solutions based on their risk profile, demonstrating a keen interest in liability insurance and a commitment to continuous learning.
* You are experienced in advising clients and have a proven track record in building strong, long-term relationships with clients.
* In previous roles, you have demonstrated strong consultative and educational skills, adept at working independently and collaboratively with a strong analytical and methodical approach.
* Your readiness to contribute whenever and wherever necessary marks your attitude.
* For the right person, there is also an opportunity to take on a leader role for the team.



  





**About Us** 




 You will work in an international environment with a local perspective, based in one of our offices in Stockholm, Malmö or Gothenburg on a hybrid basis. Based on your initiatives you will also be given opportunity for personal development. Our culture is open minded with business orientation, great team spirit, innovation and professionalism. We consider ourselves as a winning team and working with high quality towards our clients.
 



  





**WTW** 




 WTW (NASDAQ: WLTW) is a leading global insurance brokerage and risk consulting company. With roots dating back to 1828, WTW has 47,000 employees in more than 140 countries and markets.
 



 Through local presence and expertise, WTW is a global company who can offer tailor-made solutions adapted to the customer's risks and needs, local trends, markets, and legislation. Through knowledge, professionalism, commitment, and customer focus, we help customers around the world turn risk into growth opportunities.
 



  





 In Sweden, we are one of the country's top advisers in risk and insurance mediation in non-life insurance, pension and compensation solutions and actuarial services (life and property). We are approximately 200 employees in Stockholm (HQ), Gothenburg and Malmö.
 



  





**We Transform Tomorrows** 




**Equal Opportunity Employer**

title: Senior Analytics Engineer - Finance
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is on a mission: to redefine how legal work gets done. From the very start we have been very clear about the fact that we are not building a solution for lawyers, we are building it with them, because it is the only way to make sure it gets done the right way; working side-by-side every step of the way.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace empowers legal professionals not just to work faster - but to ask better questions, unlock new insights. Every day, we push the boundaries of legal tech to make complex processes smarter, faster, and more human. From thousands of documents analysed in minutes to intelligent workflows designed in collaboration with leading practices, we’re turning possibility into reality.
   

  

 Today we are trusted by global firms like Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Bird & Bird and Linklaters in over 40 countries, but we have no plans on stopping here. We ship fast, we iterate effectively, and we scale rapidly - not by accident, but by design.
   

  

 When you join Legora, you become part of a team that believes "good enough" isn’t good enough and that the way to win is together, by empowering lawyers to do their best work with technology that truly understands them. If you’re excited by building from first principles, working with exceptional people, and accelerating change in a high-stakes, high-impact domain—then this is the moment and the place.
   

  

**We’re not just shaping the future of legal tech — we’re defining it. Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
**The Role**
 As our Senior Analytics Engineer for Finance, you will own the data foundations that power how Legora runs its business. You'll build the financial data layer that sits behind every number we report, from ARR and retention to unit economics, cash flow, and board-level reporting and turn a fast-moving stream of billing, CRM, and product data into a source of truth the whole company can trust.
   

  

 This is a rare role that sits at the intersection of Finance, Engineering, and Analytics. You'll partner daily with Finance and GTM leadership, shaping how we forecast, plan, price, and report as we scale from scale-up to category-defining company. If you want your work to be read by the board, quoted in investor updates, and used to make real capital allocation decisions, this is the seat.
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing**
* Design, build, and maintain the data pipelines and warehouse models that power Legora's financial and business reporting - revenue, ARR, NRR, churn, CAC, LTV, pipeline, margin, and more.
* Build the single source of truth for SaaS metrics, integrating data from billing, CRM, ERP/accounting systems, product telemetry, and finance spreadsheets.
* Partner closely with Finance and FP&A to support forecasting, budgeting, scenario planning, and monthly close - making financial data faster, cleaner, and more trustworthy than it has any right to be at our stage.
* Own the data layer that feeds board reporting, investor updates, fundraising materials, and strategic reviews - and make sure the numbers always tie out.
* Build and maintain dashboards and automated reporting for Finance, RevOps, GTM, and the leadership team, and enable true self-service for stakeholders.
* Design tests, monitoring, and documentation that make financial data auditable, reconcilable, and reliable as we grow.
* Run deep-dive analyses on revenue, retention, pricing, and cost drivers - spotting the patterns that shape how we invest, price, and go to market.


**Who You Are**
* Solid experience in an analytics engineering, data engineering, or BI/finance-analytics role - ideally supporting a Finance, FP&A, or RevOps function.
* Strong command of SQL, dbt, Python, and a modern BI tool, with hands-on experience building on Snowflake (or a comparable cloud data warehouse).
* Deep understanding of data modeling, ELT best practices, and building systems that stay reliable and scalable as the business grows.
* Fluent in SaaS metrics (ARR, NRR, GRR, churn, CAC, LTV, payback, cohort analysis) and comfortable working with financial concepts like revenue recognition, deferred revenue, and unit economics.
* Experience wrangling data from systems like Stripe, Salesforce, HubSpot, NetSuite, or similar billing/CRM/ERP tools is a strong plus.
* A collaborative, curious mindset with the confidence to work independently, challenge assumptions, and push back on stakeholders (including senior ones) when the data says otherwise.
* Excited to partner directly with Finance, RevOps, and GTM leaders and drive real decisions through data - not just produce reports.
* But most importantly, you are someone who
+ “Gets stuff done” and understands building a $10bn company isn’t always glamorous and takes hard work and long hours
+ Strives in a fast-paced environment where the answers aren’t always clear and processes are few


**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Network Security Architect
description:
På HiQ söker vi dig som brinner för nätverk och säkerhet och som vill kombinera arkitektur och hands-on-expertis. Hos oss får du både designa och bygga nästa generations nät- och säkerhetslösningar, från målarkitektur och design till implementation, optimering och vidareutveckling.
   

  

 Tänk dig att kliva in på jobbet och växla mellan att ta fram arkitektur och design för komplexa nätverk, och att själv vara nere i konfiguration, felsökning och optimering. Våra kunders miljöer spänner över stora datacenter, hybrida plattformar och molnlösningar med gott om utrymme för teknikdjup och innovation.
   

  

**Du kommer att…**
* Arbeta brett med switching, routing och wifi i både klassiska serverhallar och moderna moln- och hybridmiljöer. Du är med och bygger robusta lösningar med SD WAN, VPN och lastbalansering och ser till att de fungerar stabilt i vardagen, inte bara på ritbordet.
* Jobba med nätverkssäkerhet på både detaljnivå och helhetsnivå – från brandväggar, IDS/IPS, proxy, loggning och PKI till hur allt hänger ihop i en säker arkitektur. Samtidigt använder du programmering och automation för att förenkla, kvalitetssäkra och utveckla nätet. Du rör dig i virtualiserade och containerbaserade miljöer som VMware och Docker/Kubernetes och får utrymme att prova ny teknik när den dyker upp.
* Arbeta med lösningar från flera leverantörer som Cisco, Juniper, HPE/Aruba, Palo Alto, Fortinet och Azure. Certifieringar är ett plus, men det som väger tyngst är din vilja att hela tiden ta nästa steg och lära dig mer.
* Utveckla och förvalta nätverkslösningar som klarar dagens krav och samtidigt förbereder kunderna för det som kommer härnäst. Du samarbetar nära både kollegor och kunder, testar idéer, utforskar ny teknik och vässar din kompetens inom allt från SD WAN och datacenter till moln och nätverkssäkerhet.


**Vi tror att du:**
* Har minst tre-fem års erfarenhet av nätverk och säkerhet i större eller komplexa miljöer, där du både arbetat praktiskt med drift/implementation och haft ett tydligt inslag av design/arkitektur.
* Trivs med att växla mellan strategiskt och operativt arbete. Du gillar att ta ansvar för hur lösningar hänger ihop, men vill också fortsätta vara nära tekniken.
* Är van att navigera mellan olika teknologier och leverantörer och kan motivera dina val, både inför tekniker och beslutsfattare.
* Talar och skriver svenska obehindrat och har en nyfikenhet som driver dig att både fördjupa din tekniska kompetens och utvecklas i en mer arkitektonisk riktning.


**Varför HiQ?**
 Sedan 1995 har HiQ hjälpt kunder och användare att göra livet enklare med teknik, design och kommunikation. Vi har alltid haft två fokus: att ha roligt på jobbet och att leverera bättre lösningar än förväntat – det är det som driver oss! Som HiQ:are är vi stolta över vår kultur där resultat, ansvar, enkelhet och glädje står i centrum. Vi tror på samarbete för att nå gemensamma mål och på att stötta varandra att växa. Här får du inte bara utveckla din egen kompetens, utan också chansen att lära av, leda och inspirera dina kollegor.
   

  

 Låter det spännande? Skicka in din ansökan och bli en del av vårt nätverk, vi ser fram emot att höra hur du vill bidra till vår gemensamma resa!

title: Control System Engineer
description:
The European Spallation Source (ESS) is one of the largest science and technology infrastructure projects being built today. Our goal is to become the brightest source of neutrons for science and technology and be operational by the end of this decade. A state-of-the-art suite of neutron scattering instruments will be made available to address a wide range of scientific fields in fundamental and materials science. Powered by the world’s most advanced linear proton accelerator, ESS will enable cutting-edge research in materials science, energy, health, and the environment for decades to come.


*Join us in shaping the future of science - from the ground up.*


**About the role:**



We are looking for a Control System Engineer to join our Hardware & Integration Group. The group is responsible for the integration of all hardware in the ESS facility into the integrated control system. This includes a diverse flora of different systems ranging from PLC systems for water flow control to fast FPGA-based data acquisition systems, all integrated towards the Experimental Physics and Industrial Control System software framework.



Collaboration is central to this role. You’ll be a part of a large and diverse control systems team with highly qualified, experienced and supportive colleagues working together during an intensive, developing commissioning phase. The team is divided into five work packages with great focus on dynamic collaboration, technical expertise and careful quality follow-ups. Together you will work to develop local control systems and integrate them with the EPICS framework.



As a Control System Engineer at ESS, you’ll play a key role in designing, implementing, and maintaining complex control systems. More braodly, this role involve will involve:


* Documenting and analysing stakeholder and internal requirements, ensuring that every solution aligns with project needs.
* Creating test and validation plans, contribute to system design, and prepare for technical reviews.
* Bring these solutions to life, as well as troubleshooting and optimising existing systems.
* Driving testing and validation procedures.
* Supporting and maintaining delivered systems will also be part of your responsibilities to ensure their long-term reliability and performance.


This is a permanent role which is based primarily “on-site” at the brand-new ESS campus in Lund, Sweden.


**About you:**



For this role, we are seeking control systems engineer with a Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in engineering, technology or science. You should be curious, motivated, and eager to grow your skills while contributing to a world‑class scientific facility where we turn diverse hardware and software systems into a coherent, reliable whole.



With a solid foundation in embedded software development, you enjoy working close to hardware. You need to have experience developing in C/C++, working in Linux environments, and familiarity with modern development practices - including Git - and contributing to and working with open‑source projects.



You have some experience with EPICS or a similar control system framework used in accelerator or large‑scale facilities (such as TANGO or other bespoke control systems), and you are interested in learning more about integrating hardware devices into a facility‑wide control system. Prior experience with neutron science is not required.



You are someone who enjoys working collaboratively and who is comfortable interacting with engineers, scientists, and other stakeholders to help define the scope of work, implement solutions, and follow through with documentation and verification. You take pride in writing clear code and clear documentation, and you value quality and reliability.



You work in a structured and responsible way, are open to feedback, and are keen to learn from more experienced colleagues. You are comfortable in an environment where systems are still being developed and commissioned, and where curiosity, initiative, and a strong safety culture are essential. Fluency in English, which is the working language at ESS, is essential. Knowledge of Swedish or other European languages would be an advantage.



Experience from large‑scale research facilities, or with controls for accelerators, neutron sources, or beamlines, would be considered a merit, as would familiarity with automation and configuration tools such as Ansible/AWX.


**To apply:**



A career at ESS gives you the opportunity to contribute to the future discoveries within neutron science at a stunning, brand-new workplace filled with innovative minds and brilliant people from across the globe. We offer market competitive, individualised compensation which is differentiated according to role, responsibilities, individual skills, abilities and achievements as well as monthly pension contributions on top of your salary.



If you see yourself in what we’re looking for, please provide your CV and motivation letter in English . Please be aware that we can only accept direct applications made via the ESS website. Submit your application as soon as you can and quote the job reference number ESD-78852 in your application.



This role requires additional background screening processes and health and safety checks, which will be performed during the recruitment process.



For further information regarding the position, please contact Talent Acquisition Specialist Daniel Nordin Baker on daniel.nordin@ess.eu or hiring manager Karl Vestin on Karl.Vestin@ess.eu



For trade union information, please contact Unionen representative Nerusha Naicker on Nerusha.Naicker@ess.eu or SACO/Swedish Association of Graduate Engineers representative Marc Kickulies at marc.kickulies@ess.eu


*We look forward to receiving your application soon!*

title: Senior Control System Engineer
description:
The European Spallation Source (ESS) is one of the largest science and technology infrastructure projects being built today. Our goal is to become the brightest source of neutrons for science and technology and be operational by the end of this decade. A state-of-the-art suite of neutron scattering instruments will be made available to address a wide range of scientific fields in fundamental and materials science. Powered by the world’s most advanced linear proton accelerator, ESS will enable cutting-edge research in materials science, energy, health, and the environment for decades to come.


*Join us in shaping the future of science - from the ground up.*


**About the role:**



We are looking for a Senior Control system Engineer to join our Hardware & Integration Group. The group is responsible for the integration of all hardware in the ESS facility into the integrated control system. This includes a diverse flora of different systems ranging from PLC systems for water flow control to fast FPGA-based data acquisition systems, all integrated towards the Experimental Physics and Industrial Control System software framework.



You will be a part of a large and diverse control systems team with highly qualified, experienced and supportive colleagues working together during an intensive, developing commissioning phase. The team is divided into five work packages with great focus on dynamic collaboration, technical expertise and careful quality follow-ups. Together you will work to develop local control systems and integrate them with the EPICS framework.


**In this role, you will:**


* Lead and supervise work to develop new control system integrations.
* Mentor and support engineers.
* Develop strategies for control system development.
* Represent ESS in the international community for controls systems for large scale research facilities.
* Take active part in development for control system integrations, EPICS modules and software technology.


This is a permanent role which is based primarily “on-site” at the brand-new ESS campus in Lund, Sweden.


**About you:**



For this role, we are seeking an experienced, senior control systems engineer who thrives on complexity and enjoys turning diverse hardware and software systems into a coherent, reliable whole. With a Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in engineering, technology or science, you are motivated by working at the forefront of large‑scale scientific infrastructure and want your expertise to have a visible, long‑term impact.



Ideally, you have a minimum of five years of prior experience in control system development and extensive hands‑on experience with EPICS. You should be confident working across the full control system stack and have a strong, well‑documented skill set in EPICS, C/C++, Python, and related software technologies. You actively contribute to control system integrations, EPICS modules, and supporting software, and you are comfortable working in an evolving commissioning environment.



For this senior engineer role, you should be well known within the controls and EPICS community with a track record of actively engaging beyond your immediate organisation. You therefore understand how to use your position at ESS to influence, contribute to, and leverage the wider EPICS ecosystem, and you have experience establishing and maintaining international collaborations within the community. Representing ESS in international forums, collaborations, and technical discussions is something you should both enjoy and excel at.



Beyond technical excellence, we’re seeking a natural technical leader, with documented experience leading and mentoring engineers, guiding development work, and helping teams deliver high‑quality solutions in demanding environments. You enjoy shaping technical direction, improving ways of working, and sharing knowledge within a collaborative and diverse team.



Experience from large‑scale research facilities, particularly in linear accelerators, neutron sources, or beamline controls, is highly valued. Familiarity with configuration management and automation tools such as Ansible/AWX would be an advantage.



Just as important as your technical background is who you are as a colleague. We’re seeking a team player with a positive, open mindset, strong problem‑solving and analytical skills, and a genuine curiosity for new challenges. You work in a structured, organised, and responsible way, with a strong commitment to safety and quality.



Fluency in spoken and written English - the working language at ESS - is required. Knowledge of Swedish or other European languages would be an asset.


**To apply:**



A career at ESS gives you the opportunity to contribute to the future discoveries within neutron science at a stunning, brand-new workplace filled with innovative minds and brilliant people from across the globe. We offer market competitive, individualised compensation which is differentiated according to role, responsibilities, individual skills, abilities and achievements as well as monthly pension contributions on top of your salary.



If you see yourself in what we’re looking for, please provide your CV and motivation letter in English . Please be aware that we can only accept direct applications made via the ESS website. Submit your application as soon as you can and quote the job reference number ESD-78853 in your application.



This role requires additional background screening processes and health and safety checks, which will be performed during the recruitment process.



For further information regarding the position, please contact Talent Acquisition Specialist Daniel Nordin Baker on daniel.nordin@ess.eu or hiring manager Karl Vestin on Karl.Vestin@ess.eu



For trade union information, please contact Unionen representative Nerusha Naicker on Nerusha.Naicker@ess.eu or SACO/Swedish Association of Graduate Engineers representative Marc Kickulies at marc.kickulies@ess.eu


*We look forward to receiving your application soon!*

title: Application Engineer
description:
Time for you—a graduate engineer in automation, mechatronics, or electrical engineering—to make a MOVE together with us! This is your opportunity to join a global company as an Application Engineer, where you will work on-site with customers to install, test, and verify AGV systems, and troubleshoot and support ongoing projects. Together, we will move the world towards easy and sustainable.


Job description


As an Application Engineer, you play a key role in designing and delivering advanced AGV and automation solutions for our customers across Europe. You will:

* Develop and simulate customer‑specific automated solutions
* Create layouts and design workflows and logical structures for AGV systems
* Perform and support with installation, testing, verification, and troubleshooting at the office location and on customer sites
* Participate in training application engineers from our European sales companies


A Strong Start — With Our Onboarding Program


You will join a structured onboarding program combining practical training and theory. Here, you learn the tools, hardware, and software used across TMHLS — supported by your assigned mentor, who follows you throughout the program and into your first projects.


At TMHLS, you fit in if you are analytical, organised, and proactive. You enjoy teamwork, adapt quickly, and naturally look for ways to improve. Maybe you are also curious about programming and technology? You are:  



* Recently graduated with an engineering degree in automation, engineering, or mechatronics, or you have a few years of relevant experience
* Comfortable with travelling within Europe and working closely with customers
* Fluent in English and Swedish
* Holding of a valid driver’s license (B)


At Toyota Material Handling Logistics Solutions, we offer more than a role — we offer a place where you can grow, thrive, and be proud of the work you do.


We are global and influential in a high-tech industry undergoing rapid transformation. Here, you will work in an international context alongside colleagues who share a passion for innovation, quality, and meaningful impact.


This position is based in our modern offices at Lindholmen — right in the heart of Gothenburg’s tech and innovation district. We also value flexibility and trust. That’s why you can work up to 50% from home.


At TMHLS, you will be surrounded by people who are not only skilled but also genuinely supportive. We believe in collaboration, learning, and celebrating achievements together. While you help move us forward, we look forward to supporting your development and giving you room to grow your career in the direction that excites you.


About our company


Toyota Material Handling Logistics Solutions (TMHLS) AB is part of Toyota Material Handling Europe, where we have 13,000 colleagues passionate about supporting companies of all sizes with today’s and tomorrow’s material handling challenges. The subsidiary Logistics Solutions, a central part of the development and innovation, is located at Lindholmen in Gothenburg. TMHLS develops solutions for warehouse automation and has approximately 80 employees.


How to apply


We look forward to your application. Please note that we will review applications continuously, so send your application today! If you have any questions, don’t hesitate to get in touch with the recruiting manager, Joakim Kjällström, at Joakim.kjallstrom@toyota-industries.eu.
